# Plan: Overview view — 3-bucket sections, compact rows, drop redundant bar ## Context PR #39 landed the Overview view with nested domain → PDT → tools containment. The user wants three follow-up refinements that together tighten the layout and reorganize the public-layer surface: 1. **Compact tool rows.** Today the status dots float to the right of the name via `ml-auto`, leaving awkward whitespace on short names. Drop the push so dots sit immediately after the tool name. 2. **3-bucket regrouping on the public layer.** The 5 existing domains (`airnd`, `prodsw`, `hardware`, `proddigi`, `infra`) map cleanly onto three named sections rendered top-to-bottom: **AI R&D**, **Product & Software**, **Hardware, Digitization & Infra**. The first two buckets each contain a single domain card; the third holds three domain cards in a horizontal sub-grid. 3. **Remove the domain-level status bar.** The thin segmented bar under the domain header duplicates the `% released` figure already in the same line — drop it to reduce visual noise and reclaim vertical space. Industry layer stays unchanged: 12 flat sector cards (the AI/Product/Other buckets are public-domain concepts and don't map to industry sectors). This is a presentation-only change — no DB, API, or shared schema changes; the existing `LayerPayload` is untouched. ## Changes ### `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` (single file edit) **Script:** - Add a `BUCKET_FOR: Record` constant mapping the 5 known public domain keys (`airnd → "rnd"`, `prodsw → "product"`, `hardware | proddigi | infra → "other"`). Unknown keys fall through to `"other"`. - Add an `OverviewBucket` interface: `{ key: "rnd" | "product" | "other", title: string, items: DomainGroup[] }`. - Add a `buckets` computed that, on the **public** layer, partitions the incoming domain groups into three `OverviewBucket`s in fixed order (`rnd`, `product`, `other`), filtering out empty buckets so a fully-filtered status query doesn't render bare section headers. - Keep helpers: `dotClass`, `mcpTitle`, `pdtMcpCount`. No need for `statsFor` (already absent). **Template — public layer (new):** - Outer wrapper switches from a single auto-fit grid to a vertical `flex flex-col gap-5` of `
`s, one per bucket. - Each bucket section gets a lightweight header: serif title (`text-[18px]`) + mono `(count)` of domains, no border-bottom — quiet inline header that doesn't compete with the inner domain-card headers. - Bucket body is a grid: `repeat(auto-fit, minmax(320px, 1fr))` for the `other` bucket (so the 3 domain cards lay out 1–3 across based on viewport), and `1fr` single-column for the `rnd` and `product` buckets so the single domain card takes the full width. - Each domain card renders the same existing inner structure (nested PDT sub-blocks → tool rows) **minus the status bar block** (see below). **Template — industry layer (unchanged behavior, sector cards stay flat):** - Iterate `props.groups` directly (no bucketing). One sector card per group, header + tool rows. **The status bar under the sector header is also removed** for consistency with the public layer. **Domain / sector header — remove the bar:** - Delete the `
…
` block that sits between the header and the body. The `% released` number stays in the header line (already there); it carries the same information more compactly. **Tool row — compact (status after name):** - In both the sector tool row and the inner PDT-block tool row, change the dots container from `
` to `
` (drop `ml-auto`), and tighten the gap between the name `` and the dots container from `gap-1.5` → `gap-1.5` (sector) / `gap-1` (PDT) — already small, but the removal of `ml-auto` is the real change. - Keep dot sizing: `size-[7px]` in sector rows, `size-[6px]` in PDT sub-block rows. - Keep the `truncate shrink` on the name `` so long names still clip rather than push dots off-row. ### `i18n/locales/en.json` and `zh.json` (3 new strings) Add under the existing `mcpPanorama` namespace: ```jsonc "bucket": { "rnd": "AI R&D", // zh: "AI 研发" "product": "Product & Software", // zh: "产品与软件" "other": "Hardware, Digitization & Infra" // zh: "硬件、数字化与基础设施" } ``` ### No other files change - `app/composables/usePanoramaState.ts` — unchanged. - `app/components/mcp-landscape/SectionHeader.vue` — unchanged (the three-button toggle is correct). - `app/pages/mcp/panorama.vue` — unchanged (still passes `filteredGroups` to `OverviewView`). - `shared/db/schema/mcp-landscape.ts`, `server/utils/queries/mcp-landscape.ts`, `server/api/internal/mcp-landscape.get.ts` — unchanged. 3-bucket grouping is presentation-only. ## Verification 1. `bun run lint` — clean. 2. `bun run typecheck` — exit 0 (baseline tooling warnings only). 3. `bun run test` — all 256 existing tests still pass; no new tests required (purely visual refinement; the bucket-mapping function is a tiny pure lookup not worth a dedicated test). 4. **Manual browser check (user-side):** `bun run dev`, then: - `/en/mcp/panorama` (public, default = overview) — confirm three labeled sections stack top-to-bottom: AI R&D (one card), Product & Software (one card), Hardware, Digitization & Infra (three cards laid out 1–3 wide depending on viewport). Confirm the segmented status bar is gone from every domain card header. - `/en/mcp/panorama?layer=industry` — confirm flat sector cards (no bucketing), and confirm the status bar is gone there too. - `/en/mcp/panorama?status=released` (or `dev` / `none`) — confirm sections that lose all their domains after filtering vanish entirely (no orphan section header). - `/zh/mcp/panorama` — confirm the three Chinese bucket labels render correctly. - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` — confirm those modes are untouched and the segmented bar inside `LayerSummary` (the regular panorama) remains intact. - Click a tool's dot → existing detail panel still opens. - Click a domain title → drill still works (URL gains `?primary=...`). ## Critical files - `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` — modified (add bucketing on public, remove status bars on both layers, tighten tool rows) - `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json` — add `mcpPanorama.bucket.{rnd,product,other}` - Reference only (no edits): `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` (domain key truth), `shared/mcp-panorama.ts` (`Group` discriminator), `app/components/mcp-landscape/LayerSummary.vue` (regular panorama's status bar — must stay intact)

Do **not** create a new branch. - Commit as a coherent unit on top of `ae54b52` — a single `feat(mcp-panorama): …` commit covering the bucket sections, the dropped status bar, and the compact tool rows, since those three changes are tightly coupled visual refinements that ship together. No `Co-Authored-By:` trailer. - **Do not push and do not open or update any PR.** The user will review the commit locally and push themselves when satisfied; once pushed, PR #39 will automatically pick up the new commit.

file.content:
# Plan: Overview view — 3-bucket sections, compact rows, drop redundant bar

## Context

PR #39 landed the Overview view with nested domain → PDT → tools containment. The user wants three follow-up refinements that together tighten the layout and reorganize the public-layer surface:

1. **Compact tool rows.** Today the status dots float to the right of the name via `ml-auto`, leaving awkward whitespace on short names. Drop the push so dots sit immediately after the tool name.
2. **3-bucket regrouping on the public layer.** The 5 existing domains (`airnd`, `prodsw`, `hardware`, `proddigi`, `infra`) map cleanly onto three named sections rendered top-to-bottom: **AI R&D**, **Product & Software**, **Hardware, Digitization & Infra**. The first two buckets each contain a single domain card; the third holds three domain cards in a horizontal sub-grid.
3. **Remove the domain-level status bar.** The thin segmented bar under the domain header duplicates the `% released` figure already in the same line — drop it to reduce visual noise and reclaim vertical space.

content.text: The existing plan is about already-shipped work. New request is a separate refinement, so I'll overwrite it after exploration.

input.description: Map AI R&D PDT count + MCP dot styles
input.prompt:
Quick targeted lookup for a Vue refactor. Report under 200 words.

I need three facts about `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` and the seed data feeding it:

1. **How many PDTs does the `airnd` domain have in the seed?** Look in `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` (and `scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts` if the data structure lives there). List each PDT's key and English label. I want to know exactly how many will need to fit horizontally on one line.

2. **Current PDT sub-block layout in OverviewView.vue** — re-confirm: what's the grid-template-columns minmax value for the PDT grid inside a domain card (I believe it's `minmax(150px, 1fr)` with `gap-2`)? Also report the horizontal padding/gap of the outer page wrapper (`px-7` etc) and the per-bucket-section width so I can do napkin math for "X PDTs on one line at typical viewport width". The user wants AI R&D to fit on one line — currently wraps to 2 lines.

3. **Current MCP dot rendering** in BOTH tool-row contexts (sector flat rows and PDT inner rows): exact class string for the dot button. I know the sizes are `size-[7px]` (sector) and `size-[6px]` (PDT) with `rounded-full`. User wants pill-shaped MCP indicators (Chinese 药丸 = pill, wider than tall, e.g. `w-3 h-1.5` rounded). I need the exact current snippet so I can plan the rewrite.

That's it — no code suggestions, just the structural facts.

content.text:
Perfect. I have all three facts.

## Summary

**1. AI R&D PDTs in seed (6 total):**
- `sysdesign` – System Design
- `devsvcs` – Development Services
- `testsvcs` – Testing Services
- `rndmaint` – R&D Maintenance
- `aiprod` – AI Production Line
- `knowsvcs` – Knowledge Services

**2. PDT grid in OverviewView.vue:**
- Grid: `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px, 1fr));` (line 147)
- Gap: `gap-2` (line 146)
- Page wrapper: `px-7` (line 104) = 28px left+right padding
- Bucket section gaps: `gap-3` on domain card grid (line 117), `gap-2` on PDT grid container

At typical 1280px viewport: 1280 − 56px (padding) = 1224px available. With `gap-3` (12px) per bucket grid and 6 PDTs at 150px min each = 900px + gaps ≈ 1050px content, fits one line. Wrapping to two lines suggests viewport squeeze or gap rendering issue.

**3. Current MCP dot rendering:**

*Sector rows (line 248):*
```
class="size-[7px] rounded-full cursor-pointer transition-transform hover:scale-[1.6]"
```
Gap between dots: `gap-[3px]` (line 243)

*PDT inner rows (line 178):*
```
class="size-[6px] rounded-full cursor-pointer transition-transform hover:scale-[1.7]"
```
Gap between dots: `gap-[2px]` (line 173)

Both use dynamic status color via `dotClass(mcp.status)` which returns `bg-(--color-status-released/dev/none)`.

2. **Current PDT sub-block layout in OverviewView.vue** — re-confirm: what's the grid-template-columns minmax value for the PDT grid inside a domain card (I believe it's `minmax(150px, 1fr)` with `gap-2`)? Also report the horizontal padding/gap of the outer page wrapper (`px-7` etc) and the per-bucket-section width so I can do napkin math for "X PDTs on one line at typical viewport width". The user wants AI R&D to fit on one line — currently wraps to 2 lines.

3. **Current MCP dot rendering** in BOTH tool-row contexts (sector flat rows and PDT inner rows): exact class string for the dot button. I know the sizes are `size-[7px]` (sector) and `size-[6px]` (PDT) with `rounded-full`. User wants pill-shaped MCP indicators (Chinese 药丸 = pill, wider than tall, e.g. `w-3 h-1.5` rounded). Perfect. I have all three facts.

## Summary

**1. AI R&D PDTs in seed (6 total):**
- `sysdesign` – System Design
- `devsvcs` – Development Services
- `testsvcs` – Testing Services
- `rndmaint` – R&D Maintenance
- `aiprod` – AI Production Line
- `knowsvcs` – Knowledge Services

**2. PDT grid in OverviewView.vue:**
- Grid: `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px, 1fr));` (line 147)
- Gap: `gap-2` (line 146)
- Page wrapper: `px-7` (line 104) = 28px left+right padding
- Bucket section gaps: `gap-3` on domain card grid (line 117), `gap-2` on PDT grid container

At typical 1280px viewport: 1280 − 56px (padding) = 1224px available. With `gap-3` (12px) per bucket grid and 6 PDTs at 150px min each = 900px + gaps ≈ 1050px content, fits one line. Wrapping to two lines suggests viewport squeeze or gap rendering issue.

**3. Current MCP dot rendering:**

```
class="size-[7px] rounded-full cursor-pointer transition-transform hover:scale-[1.6]"
```
Gap between dots: `gap-[3px]` (line 243)

*PDT inner rows (line 178):*

class="size-[6px] rounded-full cursor-pointer transition-transform hover:scale-[1.7]"
```

lastPrompt: let's add more improvements on this branch: 1. make the the PDT card more thin, then make AI R&D to be oneline (now it's two lines of PDT) 2. make the icon of MCP more wider, like a 药丸 . what do you t…

content.text:
My honest take on both:

1. **Thinner PDT cards / AI R&D on one line** — agree. AI R&D has 6 PDTs. With current `minmax(150px, 1fr)` and a browse-layout sidebar eating ~280px on a 1280px viewport, the available width is ~944px and 6 × 150 = 900 + gaps spills marginally to 2 rows. Reducing the min PDT width (to ~110px) and tightening internal padding fits 6 on one line at typical widths while still leaving tools readable; the tool-name `truncate shrink` already handles squeeze. Same change benefits the "other" bucket cards too.

2. **Pill-shaped MCP indicators** — stylistic upgrade, fits the editorial theme well. Real tradeoff: a pill takes ~2× the horizontal width of a same-height dot, so a tool with 6+ MCPs will push the name to truncate more. I'll propose modest pill ratios (sector `w-[10px] h-[4px]`, PDT `w-[8px] h-[3px]`) — still definitively pill-shaped but barely wider than the current 7px / 6px dots so we don't trade legibility of names for the new shape.

No further clarification needed; pill dimensions are easy to eyeball after the first render. Writing the plan.

input.content:
# Plan: Overview view — fit AI R&D on one line + pill-shaped MCP indicators

## Context

PR #39 (commits `ae54b52` and `e8c541a`) shipped the new Overview view with three super-bucket sections, nested PDT containment, and removed status bars. Two visual tweaks remain:

1. **AI R&D currently wraps to two rows of PDT sub-blocks.** That domain has 6 PDTs (`sysdesign`, `devsvcs`, `testsvcs`, `rndmaint`, `aiprod`, `knowsvcs`); the current `minmax(150px, 1fr)` grid plus a browse-layout sidebar consuming ~280px on a typical 1280px viewport leaves only ~944px content — 6 × 150 + gaps overshoots. Tightening the PDT min-width (and trimming internal padding) lets all 6 PDTs sit on one row at common widths while keeping tool names readable via the existing `truncate shrink`.

2. **MCP indicators are currently circular dots.** The user wants them pill-shaped ("药丸") — wider than tall with fully rounded ends — so MCPs read more like deployment artifacts and less like a status traffic light. Real tradeoff: pills are wider than dots, so tools with many MCPs push their names to truncate harder; we pick a *modest* pill ratio so the visual change is unambiguous without forcing names to disappear.

This is a pure visual tuning pass — no schema, no API, no logic change.

## Changes

### `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` (single file edit)

**PDT sub-block grid (public layer, domain card body):**
- Inner grid: change `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px, 1fr))` → `repeat(auto-fit, minmax(110px, 1fr))`. With 6 PDTs × 110px = 660px + 5 × 8px gap = 700px, AI R&D fits one row even with the sidebar.
- PDT panel padding: tighten `px-2 py-1.5` → `px-1.5 py-1` so each panel takes less horizontal real estate without losing legibility.
- Inner PDT header gap: change `gap-1.5` → `gap-1` (mono count was already small).

**MCP indicators — pill shape:**
- Sector tool row dot: replace `size-[7px] rounded-full` with `w-[10px] h-[4px] rounded-full` (2.5:1 pill, ~same vertical footprint).
- PDT tool row dot: replace `size-[6px] rounded-full` with `w-[8px] h-[3px] rounded-full` (~2.7:1 pill).
- Active-MCP ring (`ring-2 ring-(--color-ink)/40 ring-offset-1`) renders cleanly on pills — no change needed.
- Hover scale (`hover:scale-[1.6]` / `hover:scale-[1.7]`) is proportional and works for pills.
- Keep `opacity-40` placeholder treatment, status color via existing `dotClass`.

**Side effect to watch:** PDT inner tool-row dot container currently uses `gap-[2px]`. Pills are taller-aspect-ratio so they read fine at 2px gap; no change needed. Sector tool-row stays at `gap-[3px]`.

### No other files change

- No new i18n strings (status labels already exist).
- No script-side changes in OverviewView (no new computed, no new helpers).
- No other component touched. The regular Panorama view (`PanoramaView.vue` → `McpTile.vue`) keeps its existing pill-style chip rendering and is unaffected.

## Verification

1. `bun run lint` — clean.
2. `bun run typecheck` — exit 0.
3. `bun run test` — 256/256 must still pass (purely styling change).
4. **Manual browser check (user-side):** `bun run dev`, then:
   - `/en/mcp/panorama` (default = overview, public) — AI R&D bucket: all 6 PDTs (`sysdesign`, `devsvcs`, `testsvcs`, `rndmaint`, `aiprod`, `knowsvcs`) should sit in one row at viewport widths ≥ ~1200px. At narrower widths the grid will wrap naturally.
   - Confirm MCP indicators are now pills (wider than tall) in both sector and PDT contexts, with the same status colors.
   - `/en/mcp/panorama?layer=industry` — sector tool rows show the wider pill style.
   - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` — confirm those modes still use the existing detailed pill-chip rendering from `McpTile.vue` and are untouched.

## Critical files

- `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` — modified (PDT grid min-width + padding + MCP pill shape in two tool-row contexts)
- Reference only (no edits): `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` (confirms AI R&D has 6 PDTs), `app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.vue` (existing pill chip rendering — design reference)

## Commit & branch flow

- Stay on the existing branch `feat/panorama-overview-mode`. Commit as a single `style(mcp-panorama): …` commit on top of `e8c541a`, since both tweaks are visual-only and ship together. No `Co-Authored-By:` trailer.
- Do not push automatically — wait for explicit "push it" from the user, same as last round.

input.plan:
# Plan: Overview view — fit AI R&D on one line + pill-shaped MCP indicators

## Context

PR #39 (commits `ae54b52` and `e8c541a`) shipped the new Overview view with three super-bucket sections, nested PDT containment, and removed status bars. Two visual tweaks remain:

1. **AI R&D currently wraps to two rows of PDT sub-blocks.** That domain has 6 PDTs (`sysdesign`, `devsvcs`, `testsvcs`, `rndmaint`, `aiprod`, `knowsvcs`); the current `minmax(150px, 1fr)` grid plus a browse-layout sidebar consuming ~280px on a typical 1280px viewport leaves only ~944px content — 6 × 150 + gaps overshoots. Tightening the PDT min-width (and trimming internal padding) lets all 6 PDTs sit on one row at common widths while keeping tool names readable via the existing `truncate shrink`.

2. **MCP indicators are currently circular dots.** The user wants them pill-shaped ("药丸") — wider than tall with fully rounded ends — so MCPs read more like deployment artifacts and less like a status traffic light. Real tradeoff: pills are wider than dots, so tools with many MCPs push their names to truncate harder; we pick a *modest* pill ratio so the visual change is unambiguous without forcing names to disappear.

This is a pure visual tuning pass — no schema, no API, no logic change.

## Changes

### `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` (single file edit)

**PDT sub-block grid (public layer, domain card body):**
- Inner grid: change `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px, 1fr))` → `repeat(auto-fit, minmax(110px, 1fr))`. With 6 PDTs × 110px = 660px + 5 × 8px gap = 700px, AI R&D fits one row even with the sidebar.
- PDT panel padding: tighten `px-2 py-1.5` → `px-1.5 py-1` so each panel takes less horizontal real estate without losing legibility.
- Inner PDT header gap: change `gap-1.5` → `gap-1` (mono count was already small).

**MCP indicators — pill shape:**
- Sector tool row dot: replace `size-[7px] rounded-full` with `w-[10px] h-[4px] rounded-full` (2.5:1 pill, ~same vertical footprint).
- PDT tool row dot: replace `size-[6px] rounded-full` with `w-[8px] h-[3px] rounded-full` (~2.7:1 pill).
- Active-MCP ring (`ring-2 ring-(--color-ink)/40 ring-offset-1`) renders cleanly on pills — no change needed.
- Hover scale (`hover:scale-[1.6]` / `hover:scale-[1.7]`) is proportional and works for pills.
- Keep `opacity-40` placeholder treatment, status color via existing `dotClass`.

**Side effect to watch:** PDT inner tool-row dot container currently uses `gap-[2px]`. Pills are taller-aspect-ratio so they read fine at 2px gap; no change needed. Sector tool-row stays at `gap-[3px]`.

### No other files change

- No new i18n strings (status labels already exist).
- No script-side changes in OverviewView (no new computed, no new helpers).
- No other component touched. The regular Panorama view (`PanoramaView.vue` → `McpTile.vue`) keeps its existing pill-style chip rendering and is unaffected.

## Verification

1. `bun run lint` — clean.
2. `bun run typecheck` — exit 0.
3. `bun run test` — 256/256 must still pass (purely styling change).
4. **Manual browser check (user-side):** `bun run dev`, then:
   - `/en/mcp/panorama` (default = overview, public) — AI R&D bucket: all 6 PDTs (`sysdesign`, `devsvcs`, `testsvcs`, `rndmaint`, `aiprod`, `knowsvcs`) should sit in one row at viewport widths ≥ ~1200px. At narrower widths the grid will wrap naturally.
   - Confirm MCP indicators are now pills (wider than tall) in both sector and PDT contexts, with the same status colors.
   - `/en/mcp/panorama?layer=industry` — sector tool rows show the wider pill style.
   - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` — confirm those modes still use the existing detailed pill-chip rendering from `McpTile.vue` and are untouched.

## Critical files

- `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` — modified (PDT grid min-width + padding + MCP pill shape in two tool-row contexts)
- Reference only (no edits): `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` (confirms AI R&D has 6 PDTs), `app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.vue` (existing pill chip rendering — design reference)

## Commit & branch flow

- Stay on the existing branch `feat/panorama-overview-mode`. Commit as a single `style(mcp-panorama): …` commit on top of `e8c541a`, since both tweaks are visual-only and ship together. No `Co-Authored-By:` trailer.
- Do not push automatically — wait for explicit "push it" from the user, same as last round.

content.text:
Committed `3564699` on top of `e8c541a`. Local branch is now 1 commit ahead of `origin/feat/panorama-overview-mode` — nothing pushed.

## What landed

`app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` — 5 insertions / 5 deletions, three groups of edits:

- **PDT grid**: `minmax(150px, 1fr)` → `minmax(110px, 1fr)`. AI R&D's 6 PDTs fit ~700px (660 + 5×8px gaps), comfortable inside a ~944px content area on a 1280px viewport with the browse-layout sidebar.
- **PDT panel padding**: `px-2 py-1.5` → `px-1.5 py-1`; header gap `gap-1.5` → `gap-1`. Each PDT card is visibly thinner.
- **MCP pill indicators**: 
  - Sector tool row: `size-[7px] rounded-full` → `w-[10px] h-[4px] rounded-full`
  - PDT inner tool row: `size-[6px] rounded-full` → `w-[8px] h-[3px] rounded-full`

Active ring, hover scale, status color, and placeholder opacity all carry over unchanged on the new pill geometry.

## Verification

- `bun run lint` clean
- `bun run typecheck` exit 0
- `bun run test` 256/256

**For your browser check before pushing:** confirm at `≥ 1200px` viewport that AI R&D's PDT row is single-line; verify the pills read as pills (not stretched dots); spot-check that no tool name in a sector or PDT row now wraps awkwardly because of the wider indicators. When you're ready, say "push it" and I'll push the commit so PR #39 picks it up.

file.content:
# Plan: Overview view — fit AI R&D on one line + pill-shaped MCP indicators

## Context

PR #39 (commits `ae54b52` and `e8c541a`) shipped the new Overview view with three super-bucket sections, nested PDT containment, and removed status bars. Two visual tweaks remain:

1. **AI R&D currently wraps to two rows of PDT sub-blocks.** That domain has 6 PDTs (`sysdesign`, `devsvcs`, `testsvcs`, `rndmaint`, `aiprod`, `knowsvcs`); the current `minmax(150px, 1fr)` grid plus a browse-layout sidebar consuming ~280px on a typical 1280px viewport leaves only ~944px content — 6 × 150 + gaps overshoots. Tightening the PDT min-width (and trimming internal padding) lets all 6 PDTs sit on one row at common widths while keeping tool names readable via the existing `truncate shrink`.

2. **MCP indicators are currently circular dots.** The user wants them pill-shaped ("药丸") — wider than tall with fully rounded ends — so MCPs read more like deployment artifacts and less like a status traffic light. Real tradeoff: pills are wider than dots, so tools with many MCPs push their names to truncate harder; we pick a *modest* pill ratio so the visual change is unambiguous without forcing names to disappear.

This is a pure visual tuning pass — no schema, no API, no logic change.

## Changes

### `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` (single file edit)

input.prompt:
I'm planning a data re-import for the MCP Panorama (public layer only) in this Nuxt 4 marketplace. The user will provide a hierarchical text format: 领域 (domain) → PDT → 目标系统工具 (tool) → MCP. They want this re-imported, replacing existing public-layer data. I need a clear structural map of the existing seed pipeline so I can plan how the new data lands.

Report back, under 400 words, on:

1. **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts`** — full structure. What's exported? List every top-level export and its TypeScript shape (domain entries, PDT entries, tools, MCPs). I need the literal field names (`key`, `name`, `nameZh`, `blurb`, `tags`, `status`, etc.) and which ones are required vs optional. Pay particular attention to:
   - How a placeholder/in-development MCP is currently represented (the user's format will mark these as `mcp_开发中` — what fields do I need to populate to match?)
   - How tool-with-zero-MCPs is currently represented (the format implies these exist — empty `mcps` array? Or a synthesized placeholder?)
   - Whether the file already separates `industry` (sector) and `public` (domain) data — I need to know what to keep vs replace.

2. **`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`** — behavior. Is it truncate-and-insert (drops `mcp_*` tables then reseeds), upsert-on-key (idempotent), or append-only? What's the order of operations? How are PKs derived — auto-increment vs derived from the `key` slug? Does it read directly from `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` or from somewhere else? Does it touch `mcp_sectors` (industry) if I only change the domains data?

3. **`shared/db/schema/mcp-landscape.ts`** — schema for the affected tables. What are the FK relationships? List the columns for `mcp_domains`, `mcp_pdts`, `mcp_landscape_tools`, `mcp_landscape_mcps` (and any join tables). I especially need to know:
   - Which columns are NOT NULL with no default — those will need values for every new row
   - Where the `status: "released" | "dev" | "none"` lives — on the MCP, the tool, or both
   - The `layer: "industry" | "public"` column location (the previous explorer mentioned it lives on `mcp_landscape_tools.layer`).

4. **Any existing helper for parsing a text-tree format into seed data?** Search `scripts/`, `shared/`, and any dev tooling for hints. If nothing exists, just say so — I'll plan a one-off parser.

5. **Re-run command** — what npm/bun script runs the seed? Is it `bun run db:seed:mcp`? Any prerequisite (migrations, DB connection env)? Does it skip in CI or always run?

Goal: I need to know how to fit the user's incoming text-tree into the existing seed format and which fields will need defaults when the tree omits them (descriptions, slugs, tags, English translations, etc.).

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me write a clear report (under 400 words as requested):

---

## MCP Landscape Seed Pipeline Map

**1. Exported Structure from `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts`:**

- **`McpStatus`** = `"released" | "dev" | "none"`
- **`INDUSTRY_SECTORS: McpSectorSeed[]`** — 12 sectors (wireless, datacom, cloud, etc.)
  - Fields: `key` (required, PK), `label`, `labelZh`, `short`
- **`PUBLIC_DOMAINS: McpDomainSeed[]`** — 5 domains (AI R&D, Hardware, etc.) + nested PDTs
  - Fields: `key`, `label`, `labelZh`, `short`, `pdts[]`
  - PDTs: `key`, `label`, `labelZh`
- **`MCP_TOOLS: McpToolSeed[]`** — the product headers (tool owns 0–N MCPs)
  - Fields: `slug` (required), `name`, `nameZh`, `owner` (required, e.g., `"wireless"` or `"airnd.sysdesign"`), `blurb`, `blurbZh`, `mcps[]`
- **`McpSeed`** — individual MCP
  - Fields: `slug` (required, PK), `name?`, `nameZh?`, `released` (boolean), `inDev` (boolean), `depsCount`, `blurb`, `blurbZh`, `tags: string[]`

**In-dev MCPs** are represented via `inDev: true` + `released: false`. **Tools with zero MCPs** use `mcps: []` (the `noMcpTool()` helper); at runtime they render a single greyed-out placeholder tile. **Placeholder MCPs** don't exist in the seed — the frontend generates them client-side.

**2. Seed Behavior (`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`):**

Idempotent, non-destructive **upsert** — never truncates. Order:
1. Upsert `default` organization (system owner for extension stubs)
2. Upsert sectors by `key`
3. Upsert domains by `key`
4. Upsert PDTs by composite `key` (e.g., `airnd.sysdesign`)
5. Create marketplace extension stubs for all `released` MCPs (id: `mcp-{slug}`)
6. Upsert tool rows (slug is the surrogate key)
7. Upsert MCP rows (slug is the surrogate key, FK to tool via `toolId`)
8. **Prune stale MCPs/extensions** that are no longer in the canonical `MCP_TOOLS` inventory

Does NOT mutate sectors when only public data changes.

**3. Schema (`shared/db/schema/mcp-landscape.ts`):**

| Table | NOT NULL columns |
|-------|-----------------|
| `mcp_landscape_tools` | `slug`, `name`, `layer`, `blurb`, `blurbZh` |
| `mcp_landscape_mcps` | `toolId`, `slug`, `name`, `blurb`, `blurbZh`, `inDev` |

**Status location**: `mcp_landscape_mcps.extensionId` (non-null → released), `inDev` (in-dev flag). **Layer column**: `mcp_landscape_tools.layer` enum (`"industry"` \| `"public"`).

FKs: `ownerSector` → `mcpSectors.key`, `ownerDomain` → `mcpDomains.key`, `ownerPdt` → `mcpPdts.key`.

**4. Parsing helpers:**

None exist. The seed reads `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` directly. You'll write a one-off text-tree parser.

**5. Re-run command:**

```bash
bun run db:seed:mcp
```

Requires `DATABASE_URL` env var. Idempotent; safe to re-run on deploy (wired into `vercel-build`). No prerequisite cleanup — migrations must have run first.

---

**For your text-tree import**: Provide defaults for missing fields — slugs from names, English from Chinese if omitted, tags as empty array, depsCount as 0. The schema enforces no NULLs on core fields, so every tool/MCP row must carry `name`, `blurb`, `blurbZh`, and `inDev` at minimum.

input.content:
# Plan: Re-import MCP Panorama public-layer data from hierarchical text tree

## Context

The user is rewriting the entire public-layer taxonomy for the MCP Panorama. The new dataset will arrive as a Chinese hierarchical text tree:

```
- 领域 (domain)
    - PDT
        - 目标系统工具 (tool)
            - mcp           ← released MCP, has a name
            - mcp_开发中    ← in-development MCP, has no name yet
```

Rules from the user:
1. A tool may own **0, 1, or many** MCPs (no children = no MCP).
2. `mcp_开发中` is an in-development MCP without a name.
3. Per the user's answer to the scope question, this **also redefines domains and PDTs** — not just tools. Industry-layer (`sector`-owned) tools stay untouched.
4. The user provides Chinese; I translate to **concise English** (target: 1–3 words for names) for the bilingual columns.
5. Slugs are derived from the **English** translation (kebab-case, lowercase, alnum + hyphens).

This is an idempotent reseed: the existing seed script (`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`) upserts by key/slug and prunes stale rows, so rewriting `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` + running `bun run db:seed:mcp` cleanly replaces the public layer. The industry layer is untouched because no `sector`-owned tools appear in the user's tree.

## What I need from the user before execution

**The data itself.** Paste the full hierarchical tree in a follow-up message. I'll process it in one batch; partial pastes will produce a partial reseed and complicate diffing. Include any whole-domain or whole-PDT renames you want applied even if their tool children are unchanged.

## Transformation rules

### Domain entry
- `key` = `slugify(english_domain_name)` (kebab-case, e.g., `AI R&D` → `ai-rd`)
- `label` = concise English translation
- `labelZh` = original Chinese as written
- `short` = same as `label` (the existing 5 domains use `short` for sidebar/breadcrumb abbreviations; if a translation is already short, reuse it)
- `pdts: PdtSeed[]` = list of PDT entries

### PDT entry
- `key` = composite `{domain-key}.{pdt-slug}` (matches existing convention; e.g., `ai-rd.dev-services`)
- `label` = concise English translation
- `labelZh` = original Chinese

### Tool entry (lives in `MCP_TOOLS`)
- `slug` = `slugify(english_tool_name)`; on collision across the whole `MCP_TOOLS` array, suffix with the PDT slug (e.g., `logging-dev-services`) — slugs must be globally unique.
- `name` = concise English translation
- `nameZh` = original Chinese
- `owner` = the PDT composite key (`{domain-key}.{pdt-slug}`)
- `blurb` = `""` (schema is NOT NULL but empty string is allowed; user didn't provide blurbs)
- `blurbZh` = `""`
- `mcps` = MCP entries (empty array if the tool has no children → frontend renders a `none`-status placeholder client-side)

### MCP entry
For a named `mcp <name>` line:
- `slug` = `slugify(english_mcp_name)`; on collision, suffix with the tool slug
- `name` = concise English translation
- `nameZh` = original Chinese
- `released: true`, `inDev: false`
- `depsCount: 0`, `tags: []`, `blurb: ""`, `blurbZh: ""`

For an `mcp_开发中` line:
- `slug` = `dev-{tool-slug}-{n}` where `n` is the 1-indexed position among the tool's `mcp_开发中` siblings
- `name` = `"In Development"`
- `nameZh` = `"开发中"`
- `released: false`, `inDev: true`
- `depsCount: 0`, `tags: []`, `blurb: ""`, `blurbZh: ""`

## Files to modify

1. **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts`** — fully rewrite the `PUBLIC_DOMAINS` array and replace all `MCP_TOOLS` entries whose `owner` is a public-layer key (i.e., contains a `.`). Keep `INDUSTRY_SECTORS` and all sector-owned tools (`owner` not containing `.`) untouched.

2. **`app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue`** — update the `BUCKET_FOR` constant (lines 34–38). With re-keyed domains, the existing entries (`airnd`, `prodsw`, `hardware`, `proddigi`, `infra`) become stale. The three buckets stay (`rnd`, `product`, `other`); I'll re-map each new domain key into the appropriate bucket. Specifically:
   - The user's "AI R&D" equivalent → `rnd`
   - The user's "Product & Software" equivalent → `product`
   - Every other new domain → `other`
   - Unknown keys still fall through to `other` (existing fallback), so the panel won't blank out if the user's tree disagrees with my mapping.

3. **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.test.ts:47`** — fixture asserts `ownerToParts("airnd.devsvcs")` returns `{ primary: "airnd", secondary: "devsvcs" }`. Replace the literal with whatever the new equivalent owner string is (e.g., `ai-rd.dev-services`). The assertion stays structurally identical.

4. **`app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.nuxt.test.ts:18`** — mock factory sets `ownerPrimary: "airnd", ownerSecondary: "devsvcs"`. Replace with the new equivalent. The test only cares that the owner fields exist, not their literal value.

## Seed pipeline run

After the file rewrite:

```bash
bun run db:seed:mcp
```

The seed script (`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`) is idempotent upsert-by-key with a final prune step. Expected behavior:
- Old domain rows whose `key` no longer appears in the new `PUBLIC_DOMAINS` → pruned by FK cascade or by the explicit prune in the script.
- Old tool rows whose `slug` is no longer in `MCP_TOOLS` → pruned.
- Old marketplace extension stubs (`mcp-{slug}`) tied to dropped MCPs → pruned.
- Industry sectors / tools → unchanged.

Requires `DATABASE_URL` env. If I can't reach the dev DB from here, I'll prepare the file rewrite and ask the user to run the seed locally.

## Verification

1. `bun run lint` — clean.
2. `bun run typecheck` — exit 0.
3. `bun run test` — 256/256 (after fixture updates in step 3 and 4 above).
4. **Seed run** — `bun run db:seed:mcp` exits 0; spot-check stdout for the expected counts (N domains inserted, M tools, K MCPs, P pruned).
5. **Manual browser check (user-side):** `bun run dev`, then:
   - `/zh/mcp/panorama` (overview, public, zh locale) — confirm the new Chinese domain titles appear in the three super-buckets; PDT sub-blocks reflect the new structure; each tool's MCPs render as pills.
   - `/en/mcp/panorama` — same, in concise English.
   - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` — confirm those modes render the new taxonomy correctly (they share the same `LayerPayload`).
   - Click any in-dev (`开发中`) pill → detail panel opens, shows `inDev: true` state.
   - Click any released pill → detail panel opens with link to the marketplace stub.

## Branch & commit flow

- **New branch.** This data re-import is logically separate from the Overview-view feature PR (#39, `feat/panorama-overview-mode`). It rewrites the entire public taxonomy, affecting the regular Panorama and List views too — not just the new Overview component. Recommend a fresh branch `chore/reseed-public-mcp-landscape` off `main` so PR #39 can ship as a pure UI feature without being held up by data-review.
- **If user prefers to bundle on the current branch:** I'll do that, but flag in the commit message that this is a data refresh, not an Overview tweak.
- **Single commit** for the rewrite + 3 downstream updates (`OverviewView.vue` BUCKET_FOR, two test fixtures) since they must ship together to keep the build green. Conventional commit: `chore(mcp-landscape): re-import public-layer taxonomy from new source-of-truth` (or `feat(...)` if the user prefers — purely a labeling call).
- **Seed run is a separate step** the user invokes against their DB; the commit only contains the source-of-truth file + downstream patches.
- No `Co-Authored-By:` trailer.
- Do not push or open a PR until the user explicitly says so.

## Critical files

- `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` — rewrite (public-layer entries)
- `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` — update `BUCKET_FOR` map
- `shared/data/mcp-landscape.test.ts` — fixture refresh
- `app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.nuxt.test.ts` — fixture refresh
- Reference only: `scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts` (upsert + prune behavior), `shared/db/schema/mcp-landscape.ts` (column constraints)

## What happens next

1. User approves this plan.
2. User pastes the hierarchical text tree (one message, full payload).
3. I parse it, apply the transformation rules, propose the translated English labels back to the user for any non-obvious term (e.g., domain-level names where translation choice matters for the URL slug) — corrections cheap before the seed run.
4. I rewrite `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` and the 3 downstream files.
5. Validate locally (lint, typecheck, test).
6. Commit. User runs the seed against their DB.

- Reference only: `scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts` (upsert + prune behavior), `shared/db/schema/mcp-landscape.ts` (column constraints)

## What happens next

1. User approves this plan.
2. User pastes the hierarchical text tree (one message, full payload).
3. I parse it, apply the transformation rules, propose the translated English labels back to the user for any non-obvious term (e.g., domain-level names where translation choice matters for the URL slug) — corrections cheap before the seed run.

# Plan: Overview view — fit AI R&D on one line + pill-shaped MCP indicators

## Context

PR #39 (commits `ae54b52` and `e8c541a`) shipped the new Overview view with three super-bucket sections, nested PDT containment, and removed status bars. Two visual tweaks remain:

1. **AI R&D currently wraps to two rows of PDT sub-blocks.** That domain has 6 PDTs (`sysdesign`, `devsvcs`, `testsvcs`, `rndmaint`, `aiprod`, `knowsvcs`); the current `minmax(150px, 1fr)` grid plus a browse-layout sidebar consuming ~280px on a typical 1280px viewport leaves only ~944px content — 6 × 150 + gaps overshoots. Tightening the PDT min-width (and trimming internal padding) lets all 6 PDTs sit on one row at common widths while keeping tool names readable via the existing `truncate shrink`.

2. **MCP indicators are currently circular dots.** The user wants them pill-shaped ("药丸") — wider than tall with fully rounded ends — so MCPs read more like deployment artifacts and less like a status traffic light. Real tradeoff: pills are wider than dots, so tools with many MCPs push their names to truncate harder; we pick a *modest* pill ratio so the visual change is unambiguous without forcing names to disappear.

This is a pure visual tuning pass — no schema, no API, no logic change.

## Changes

### `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` (single file edit)

**PDT sub-block grid (public layer, domain card body):**
- Inner grid: change `grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(150px, 1fr))` → `repeat(auto-fit, minmax(110px, 1fr))`. With 6 PDTs × 110px = 660px + 5 × 8px gap = 700px, AI R&D fits one row even with the sidebar.
- PDT panel padding: tighten `px-2 py-1.5` → `px-1.5 py-1` so each panel takes less horizontal real estate without losing legibility.
- Inner PDT header gap: change `gap-1.5` → `gap-1` (mono count was already small).

**MCP indicators — pill shape:**
- Sector tool row dot: replace `size-[7px] rounded-full` with `w-[10px] h-[4px] rounded-full` (2.5:1 pill, ~same vertical footprint).
- PDT tool row dot: replace `size-[6px] rounded-full` with `w-[8px] h-[3px] rounded-full` (~2.7:1 pill).
- Active-MCP ring (`ring-2 ring-(--color-ink)/40 ring-offset-1`) renders cleanly on pills — no change needed.
- Hover scale (`hover:scale-[1.6]` / `hover:scale-[1.7]`) is proportional and works for pills.
- Keep `opacity-40` placeholder treatment, status color via existing `dotClass`.

**Side effect to watch:** PDT inner tool-row dot container currently uses `gap-[2px]`. Pills are taller-aspect-ratio so they read fine at 2px gap; no change needed. Sector tool-row stays at `gap-[3px]`.

### No other files change

- No new i18n strings (status labels already exist).
- No script-side changes in OverviewView (no new computed, no new helpers).
- No other component touched. The regular Panorama view (`PanoramaView.vue` → `McpTile.vue`) keeps its existing pill-style chip rendering and is unaffected.

## Verification

1. `bun run lint` — clean.
2. `bun run typecheck` — exit 0.
3. `bun run test` — 256/256 must still pass (purely styling change).
4. **Manual browser check (user-side):** `bun run dev`, then:
   - `/en/mcp/panorama` (default = overview, public) — AI R&D bucket: all 6 PDTs (`sysdesign`, `devsvcs`, `testsvcs`, `rndmaint`, `aiprod`, `knowsvcs`) should sit in one row at viewport widths ≥ ~1200px. At narrower widths the grid will wrap naturally.
   - Confirm MCP indicators are now pills (wider than tall) in both sector and PDT contexts, with the same status colors.
   - `/en/mcp/panorama?layer=industry` — sector tool rows show the wider pill style.
   - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` — confirm those modes still use the existing detailed pill-chip rendering from `McpTile.vue` and are untouched.

## Critical files

- `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` — modified (PDT grid min-width + padding + MCP pill shape in two tool-row contexts)
- Reference only (no edits): `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` (confirms AI R&D has 6 PDTs), `app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.vue` (existing pill chip rendering — design reference)

## Commit & branch flow

- Stay on the existing branch `feat/panorama-overview-mode`. Commit as a single `style(mcp-panorama): …` commit on top of `e8c541a`, since both tweaks are visual-only and ship together. No `Co-Authored-By:` trailer.
- Do not push automatically — wait for explicit "push it" from the user, same as last round.

input.plan:
# Plan: Re-import MCP Panorama public-layer data from hierarchical text tree

## Context

The user is rewriting the entire public-layer taxonomy for the MCP Panorama. The new dataset will arrive as a Chinese hierarchical text tree:

```
- 领域 (domain)
    - PDT
        - 目标系统工具 (tool)
            - mcp           ← released MCP, has a name
            - mcp_开发中    ← in-development MCP, has no name yet
```

Rules from the user:
1. A tool may own **0, 1, or many** MCPs (no children = no MCP).
2. `mcp_开发中` is an in-development MCP without a name.
3. Per the user's answer to the scope question, this **also redefines domains and PDTs** — not just tools. Industry-layer (`sector`-owned) tools stay untouched.
4. The user provides Chinese; I translate to **concise English** (target: 1–3 words for names) for the bilingual columns.
5. Slugs are derived from the **English** translation (kebab-case, lowercase, alnum + hyphens).

This is an idempotent reseed: the existing seed script (`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`) upserts by key/slug and prunes stale rows, so rewriting `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` + running `bun run db:seed:mcp` cleanly replaces the public layer. The industry layer is untouched because no `sector`-owned tools appear in the user's tree.

## What I need from the user before execution

**The data itself.** Paste the full hierarchical tree in a follow-up message. I'll process it in one batch; partial pastes will produce a partial reseed and complicate diffing. Include any whole-domain or whole-PDT renames you want applied even if their tool children are unchanged.

## Transformation rules

### Domain entry
- `key` = `slugify(english_domain_name)` (kebab-case, e.g., `AI R&D` → `ai-rd`)
- `label` = concise English translation
- `labelZh` = original Chinese as written
- `short` = same as `label` (the existing 5 domains use `short` for sidebar/breadcrumb abbreviations; if a translation is already short, reuse it)
- `pdts: PdtSeed[]` = list of PDT entries

### PDT entry
- `key` = composite `{domain-key}.{pdt-slug}` (matches existing convention; e.g., `ai-rd.dev-services`)
- `label` = concise English translation
- `labelZh` = original Chinese

### Tool entry (lives in `MCP_TOOLS`)
- `slug` = `slugify(english_tool_name)`; on collision across the whole `MCP_TOOLS` array, suffix with the PDT slug (e.g., `logging-dev-services`) — slugs must be globally unique.
- `name` = concise English translation
- `nameZh` = original Chinese
- `owner` = the PDT composite key (`{domain-key}.{pdt-slug}`)
- `blurb` = `""` (schema is NOT NULL but empty string is allowed; user didn't provide blurbs)
- `blurbZh` = `""`
- `mcps` = MCP entries (empty array if the tool has no children → frontend renders a `none`-status placeholder client-side)

### MCP entry
For a named `mcp <name>` line:
- `slug` = `slugify(english_mcp_name)`; on collision, suffix with the tool slug
- `name` = concise English translation
- `nameZh` = original Chinese
- `released: true`, `inDev: false`
- `depsCount: 0`, `tags: []`, `blurb: ""`, `blurbZh: ""`

For an `mcp_开发中` line:
- `slug` = `dev-{tool-slug}-{n}` where `n` is the 1-indexed position among the tool's `mcp_开发中` siblings
- `name` = `"In Development"`
- `nameZh` = `"开发中"`
- `released: false`, `inDev: true`
- `depsCount: 0`, `tags: []`, `blurb: ""`, `blurbZh: ""`

## Files to modify

1. **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts`** — fully rewrite the `PUBLIC_DOMAINS` array and replace all `MCP_TOOLS` entries whose `owner` is a public-layer key (i.e., contains a `.`). Keep `INDUSTRY_SECTORS` and all sector-owned tools (`owner` not containing `.`) untouched.

2. **`app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue`** — update the `BUCKET_FOR` constant (lines 34–38). With re-keyed domains, the existing entries (`airnd`, `prodsw`, `hardware`, `proddigi`, `infra`) become stale. The three buckets stay (`rnd`, `product`, `other`); I'll re-map each new domain key into the appropriate bucket. Specifically:
   - The user's "AI R&D" equivalent → `rnd`
   - The user's "Product & Software" equivalent → `product`
   - Every other new domain → `other`
   - Unknown keys still fall through to `other` (existing fallback), so the panel won't blank out if the user's tree disagrees with my mapping.

3. **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.test.ts:47`** — fixture asserts `ownerToParts("airnd.devsvcs")` returns `{ primary: "airnd", secondary: "devsvcs" }`. Replace the literal with whatever the new equivalent owner string is (e.g., `ai-rd.dev-services`). The assertion stays structurally identical.

4. **`app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.nuxt.test.ts:18`** — mock factory sets `ownerPrimary: "airnd", ownerSecondary: "devsvcs"`. Replace with the new equivalent. The test only cares that the owner fields exist, not their literal value.

## Seed pipeline run

After the file rewrite:

```bash
bun run db:seed:mcp
```

The seed script (`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`) is idempotent upsert-by-key with a final prune step. Expected behavior:
- Old domain rows whose `key` no longer appears in the new `PUBLIC_DOMAINS` → pruned by FK cascade or by the explicit prune in the script.
- Old tool rows whose `slug` is no longer in `MCP_TOOLS` → pruned.
- Old marketplace extension stubs (`mcp-{slug}`) tied to dropped MCPs → pruned.
- Industry sectors / tools → unchanged.

Requires `DATABASE_URL` env. If I can't reach the dev DB from here, I'll prepare the file rewrite and ask the user to run the seed locally.

## Verification

1. `bun run lint` — clean.
2. `bun run typecheck` — exit 0.
3. `bun run test` — 256/256 (after fixture updates in step 3 and 4 above).
4. **Seed run** — `bun run db:seed:mcp` exits 0; spot-check stdout for the expected counts (N domains inserted, M tools, K MCPs, P pruned).
5. **Manual browser check (user-side):** `bun run dev`, then:
   - `/zh/mcp/panorama` (overview, public, zh locale) — confirm the new Chinese domain titles appear in the three super-buckets; PDT sub-blocks reflect the new structure; each tool's MCPs render as pills.
   - `/en/mcp/panorama` — same, in concise English.
   - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` — confirm those modes render the new taxonomy correctly (they share the same `LayerPayload`).
   - Click any in-dev (`开发中`) pill → detail panel opens, shows `inDev: true` state.
   - Click any released pill → detail panel opens with link to the marketplace stub.

## Branch & commit flow

- **New branch.** This data re-import is logically separate from the Overview-view feature PR (#39, `feat/panorama-overview-mode`). It rewrites the entire public taxonomy, affecting the regular Panorama and List views too — not just the new Overview component. Recommend a fresh branch `chore/reseed-public-mcp-landscape` off `main` so PR #39 can ship as a pure UI feature without being held up by data-review.
- **If user prefers to bundle on the current branch:** I'll do that, but flag in the commit message that this is a data refresh, not an Overview tweak.
- **Single commit** for the rewrite + 3 downstream updates (`OverviewView.vue` BUCKET_FOR, two test fixtures) since they must ship together to keep the build green. Conventional commit: `chore(mcp-landscape): re-import public-layer taxonomy from new source-of-truth` (or `feat(...)` if the user prefers — purely a labeling call).
- **Seed run is a separate step** the user invokes against their DB; the commit only contains the source-of-truth file + downstream patches.
- No `Co-Authored-By:` trailer.
- Do not push or open a PR until the user explicitly says so.

## Critical files

- `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` — rewrite (public-layer entries)
- `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` — update `BUCKET_FOR` map
- `shared/data/mcp-landscape.test.ts` — fixture refresh
- `app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.nuxt.test.ts` — fixture refresh
- Reference only: `scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts` (upsert + prune behavior), `shared/db/schema/mcp-landscape.ts` (column constraints)

## What happens next

1. User approves this plan.
2. User pastes the hierarchical text tree (one message, full payload).
3. I parse it, apply the transformation rules, propose the translated English labels back to the user for any non-obvious term (e.g., domain-level names where translation choice matters for the URL slug) — corrections cheap before the seed run.
4. I rewrite `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` and the 3 downstream files.
5. Validate locally (lint, typecheck, test).
6. Commit. User runs the seed against their DB.

**Special rule — tools with no MCPs are renamed.** If a tool line has no MCP children, both `name` and `nameZh` are rewritten to a *similar but not identical* paraphrase before slug derivation. The intent is to keep the semantic domain readable while making the surface text intentionally generic — typical transformations:

- Replace proprietary/internal acronyms with generic equivalents (e.g., `IPD` → `R&D`).
- Drop overly specific suffixes (`服务`, `系统`, `平台`, "Service", "System", "Platform") when they don't carry meaning.
- Keep the core technical/domain noun intact.

Example from the user: `IPD 质量管理服务` → `R&D 质量管理` (rename applied to `nameZh`; English translation flows from the renamed Chinese, e.g., `R&D Quality Management`). Slug is derived from the *renamed* English (`rd-quality-management`), not the original. Tools **with** MCPs keep their original naming verbatim.

Because this rule is judgment-based, I'll list every applied rename in the post-transform summary before running the seed so the user can correct any awkward paraphrases before they hit the DB.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Plan: Re-import MCP Panorama public-layer data from hierarchical text tree

## Context

The user is rewriting the entire public-layer taxonomy for the MCP Panorama. The new dataset will arrive as a Chinese hierarchical text tree:

```
- 领域 (domain)
    - PDT
        - 目标系统工具 (tool)
            - mcp           ← released MCP, has a name
            - mcp_开发中    ← in-development MCP, has no name yet
```

Rules from the user:
1. A tool may own **0, 1, or many** MCPs (no children = no MCP).
2. `mcp_开发中` is an in-development MCP without a name.
3. Per the user's answer to the scope question, this **also redefines domains and PDTs** — not just tools. Industry-layer (`sector`-owned) tools stay untouched.
4. The user provides Chinese; I translate to **concise English** (target: 1–3 words for names) for the bilingual columns.
5. Slugs are derived from the **English** translation (kebab-case, lowercase, alnum + hyphens).

This is an idempotent reseed: the existing seed script (`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`) upserts by key/slug and prunes stale rows, so rewriting `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` + running `bun run db:seed:mcp` cleanly replaces the public layer. The industry layer is untouched because no `sector`-owned tools appear in the user's tree.

## What I need from the user before execution

**The data itself.** Paste the full hierarchical tree in a follow-up message. I'll process it in one batch; partial pastes will produce a partial reseed and complicate diffing. Include any whole-domain or whole-PDT renames you want applied even if their tool children are unchanged.

## Transformation rules

### Domain entry
- `key` = `slugify(english_domain_name)` (kebab-case, e.g., `AI R&D` → `ai-rd`)
- `label` = concise English translation
- `labelZh` = original Chinese as written
- `short` = same as `label` (the existing 5 domains use `short` for sidebar/breadcrumb abbreviations; if a translation is already short, reuse it)
- `pdts: PdtSeed[]` = list of PDT entries

### PDT entry
- `key` = composite `{domain-key}.{pdt-slug}` (matches existing convention; e.g., `ai-rd.dev-services`)
- `label` = concise English translation
- `labelZh` = original Chinese

### Tool entry (lives in `MCP_TOOLS`)
- `slug` = `slugify(english_tool_name)`; on collision across the whole `MCP_TOOLS` array, suffix with the PDT slug (e.g., `logging-dev-services`) — slugs must be globally unique.
- `name` = concise English translation
- `nameZh` = original Chinese
- `owner` = the PDT composite key (`{domain-key}.{pdt-slug}`)
- `blurb` = `""` (schema is NOT NULL but empty string is allowed; user didn't provide blurbs)
- `blurbZh` = `""`
- `mcps` = MCP entries (empty array if the tool has no children → frontend renders a `none`-status placeholder client-side)

### MCP entry
For a named `mcp <name>` line:
- `slug` = `slugify(english_mcp_name)`; on collision, suffix with the tool slug
- `name` = concise English translation
- `nameZh` = original Chinese
- `released: true`, `inDev: false`
- `depsCount: 0`, `tags: []`, `blurb: ""`, `blurbZh: ""`

For an `mcp_开发中` line:
- `slug` = `dev-{tool-slug}-{n}` where `n` is the 1-indexed position among the tool's `mcp_开发中` siblings
- `name` = `"In Development"`
- `nameZh` = `"开发中"`
- `released: false`, `inDev: true`
- `depsCount: 0`, `tags: []`, `blurb: ""`, `blurbZh: ""`

## Files to modify

1. **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts`** — fully rewrite the `PUBLIC_DOMAINS` array and replace all `MCP_TOOLS` entries whose `owner` is a public-layer key (i.e., contains a `.`). Keep `INDUSTRY_SECTORS` and all sector-owned tools (`owner` not containing `.`) untouched.

2. **`app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue`** — update the `BUCKET_FOR` constant (lines 34–38). With re-keyed domains, the existing entries (`airnd`, `prodsw`, `hardware`, `proddigi`, `infra`) become stale. The three buckets stay (`rnd`, `product`, `other`); I'll re-map each new domain key into the appropriate bucket. Specifically:
   - The user's "AI R&D" equivalent → `rnd`
   - The user's "Product & Software" equivalent → `product`
   - Every other new domain → `other`
   - Unknown keys still fall through to `other` (existing fallback), so the panel won't blank out if the user's tree disagrees with my mapping.

3. **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.test.ts:47`** — fixture asserts `ownerToParts("airnd.devsvcs")` returns `{ primary: "airnd", secondary: "devsvcs" }`. Replace the literal with whatever the new equivalent owner string is (e.g., `ai-rd.dev-services`). The assertion stays structurally identical.

4. **`app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.nuxt.test.ts:18`** — mock factory sets `ownerPrimary: "airnd", ownerSecondary: "devsvcs"`. Replace with the new equivalent. The test only cares that the owner fields exist, not their literal value.

## Seed pipeline run

After the file rewrite:

```bash
bun run db:seed:mcp
```

The seed script (`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`) is idempotent upsert-by-key with a final prune step. Expected behavior:
- Old domain rows whose `key` no longer appears in the new `PUBLIC_DOMAINS` → pruned by FK cascade or by the explicit prune in the script.
- Old tool rows whose `slug` is no longer in `MCP_TOOLS` → pruned.
- Old marketplace extension stubs (`mcp-{slug}`) tied to dropped MCPs → pruned.
- Industry sectors / tools → unchanged.

Requires `DATABASE_URL` env. If I can't reach the dev DB from here, I'll prepare the file rewrite and ask the user to run the seed locally.

## Verification

1. `bun run lint` — clean.
2. `bun run typecheck` — exit 0.
3. `bun run test` — 256/256 (after fixture updates in step 3 and 4 above).
4. **Seed run** — `bun run db:seed:mcp` exits 0; spot-check stdout for the expected counts (N domains inserted, M tools, K MCPs, P pruned).
5. **Manual browser check (user-side):** `bun run dev`, then:
   - `/zh/mcp/panorama` (overview, public, zh locale) — confirm the new Chinese domain titles appear in the three super-buckets; PDT sub-blocks reflect the new structure; each tool's MCPs render as pills.
   - `/en/mcp/panorama` — same, in concise English.
   - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` — confirm those modes render the new taxonomy correctly (they share the same `LayerPayload`).
   - Click any in-dev (`开发中`) pill → detail panel opens, shows `inDev: true` state.
   - Click any released pill → detail panel opens with link to the marketplace stub.

## Branch & commit flow

- **New branch.** This data re-import is logically separate from the Overview-view feature PR (#39, `feat/panorama-overview-mode`). It rewrites the entire public taxonomy, affecting the regular Panorama and List views too — not just the new Overview component. Recommend a fresh branch `chore/reseed-public-mcp-landscape` off `main` so PR #39 can ship as a pure UI feature without being held up by data-review.
- **If user prefers to bundle on the current branch:** I'll do that, but flag in the commit message that this is a data refresh, not an Overview tweak.
- **Single commit** for the rewrite + 3 downstream updates (`OverviewView.vue` BUCKET_FOR, two test fixtures) since they must ship together to keep the build green. Conventional commit: `chore(mcp-landscape): re-import public-layer taxonomy from new source-of-truth` (or `feat(...)` if the user prefers — purely a labeling call).
- **Seed run is a separate step** the user invokes against their DB; the commit only contains the source-of-truth file + downstream patches.
- No `Co-Authored-By:` trailer.
- Do not push or open a PR until the user explicitly says so.

## Critical files

- `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` — rewrite (public-layer entries)
- `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` — update `BUCKET_FOR` map
- `shared/data/mcp-landscape.test.ts` — fixture refresh
- `app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.nuxt.test.ts` — fixture refresh
- Reference only: `scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts` (upsert + prune behavior), `shared/db/schema/mcp-landscape.ts` (column constraints)

## What happens next

1. User approves this plan.
2. User pastes the hierarchical text tree (one message, full payload).
3. I parse it, apply the transformation rules, propose the translated English labels back to the user for any non-obvious term (e.g., domain-level names where translation choice matters for the URL slug) — corrections cheap before the seed run.
4. I rewrite `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` and the 3 downstream files.
5. Validate locally (lint, typecheck, test).
6. Commit. User runs the seed against their DB.

Parsed. Here's my read of the tree before I commit to a plan update:

- **7 domains** (one trailing `-` line at the bottom looks accidental and will be ignored)
- **~22 PDTs**
- **~50 tools** — split between MCP-bearing (which keep their original names) and MCP-less (~30, all requiring paraphrase rename)
- **19 named MCPs** (e.g., `dbdesigner_mcp`, `cida_mcp`, `swbom_mcp`)
- **9 `mcp_开发中` placeholders** (in-dev, slug auto-generated, name = "开发中")

A few decisions I'm folding into the plan rather than asking about (you can override after seeing the diff):

1. **MCP slug normalization** — the user's identifiers like `dbdesigner_mcp`, `dts-mcp`, `ipd-rag` get normalized: underscores → hyphens, `_mcp`/`-mcp` suffix dropped (it's implicit in the table). So `dbdesigner_mcp` → slug `dbdesigner`, `dts-mcp` → `dts`, `ipd-rag` → `ipd-rag` (unchanged). Display names derived per-MCP (e.g., `dbdesigner` → "DB Designer" / "数据库设计器").
2. **Bucket mapping for `OverviewView.vue`** (rnd/product/other) — `AI 研发` → `rnd`; `产品与软件工程数字化` → `product`; remaining 5 → `other`. The "other" bucket will hold 5 domain cards and will wrap to 2 rows; acceptable density. `产品配置` is product-adjacent but stays in "other" — say so explicitly if you want it grouped with `product`.
3. **Renames for no-MCP tools** — I'll generate paraphrases per the rule, biased toward dropping generic suffixes (`服务`, `中心`) and swapping internal acronyms (`IPD` → `R&D` style). I'll output the full rename table for your review before the seed runs, not after.

Let me record these in the plan and ask one last thing about branch placement.

The user has provided the tree. Summary of the parse:

- **7 domains:** `AI 研发`, `产品与软件工程数字化`, `硬件工程数字化`, `产品数字化`, `研发设施与环境管理`, `IPD 管理与治理`, `产品配置` (the trailing `-` line in the input is treated as accidental and ignored).
- **~22 PDTs.**
- **~50 tools** — ~20 carry MCPs (kept verbatim), ~30 carry no MCPs (rename rule applied).
- **19 named MCPs** with user-supplied identifiers (e.g., `dbdesigner_mcp`, `cida_mcp`, `swbom_mcp`).
- **9 `mcp_开发中` placeholders** marked in-dev.

## Resolved decisions (added after seeing the data)

1. **MCP slug normalization.** User identifiers like `dbdesigner_mcp`, `dts-mcp`, `ipd-rag` are normalized: underscores → hyphens, trailing `_mcp` / `-mcp` suffix dropped (the table name already implies "mcp"). So `dbdesigner_mcp` → slug `dbdesigner`, `dts-mcp` → `dts`, `ipd-rag` → `ipd-rag`. Display name derived per-MCP (e.g., `dbdesigner` → "DB Designer" / "数据库设计器", `swbom` → "Software BOM" / "软件 BOM").

2. **3-bucket assignment for `OverviewView.vue` `BUCKET_FOR`:**
   - `rnd` bucket: `AI 研发`
   - `product` bucket: `产品与软件工程数字化`
   - `other` bucket: `硬件工程数字化`, `产品数字化`, `研发设施与环境管理`, `IPD 管理与治理`, `产品配置`
   Note: the "other" bucket now holds 5 domain cards (vs 3 today) and will wrap to 2 rows in the auto-fit grid on typical viewports — acceptable density.

3. **Rename review step.** Before the seed runs, I'll emit a markdown table of every no-MCP tool with `Original Chinese | Renamed Chinese | English`. The user sanity-checks the table; I correct any awkward paraphrases; then commit. This catches bad translations cheaply.

4. **Branch:** stay on `feat/panorama-overview-mode`, consistent with how the user has been treating this branch as the rolling iteration line. If on review they'd rather isolate the data work, the commit can be cherry-picked to a new branch — but defaulting to the current branch keeps the workflow simple.

The user is rewriting the entire public-layer taxonomy for the MCP Panorama. The new dataset will arrive as a Chinese hierarchical text tree:

- 领域 (domain)
    - PDT
        - 目标系统工具 (tool)
            - mcp           ← released MCP, has a name
            - mcp_开发中    ← in-development MCP, has no name yet
```

Rules from the user:
1. A tool may own **0, 1, or many** MCPs (no children = no MCP).
2. `mcp_开发中` is an in-development MCP without a name.
3. Per the user's answer to the scope question, this **also redefines domains and PDTs** — not just tools. Industry-layer (`sector`-owned) tools stay untouched.
4. The user provides Chinese; I translate to **concise English** (target: 1–3 words for names) for the bilingual columns.
5. Slugs are derived from the **English** translation (kebab-case, lowercase, alnum + hyphens).

   - `rnd` bucket: `AI 研发`
   - `product` bucket: `产品与软件工程数字化`
   - `other` bucket: `硬件工程数字化`, `产品数字化`, `研发设施与环境管理`, `产品配置`
   Four cards in the "other" bucket fit comfortably in one row at typical viewport widths.

3. **Rename review step.** Before the seed runs, I'll emit a markdown table of every no-MCP tool with `Original Chinese | Renamed Chinese | English`. The user sanity-checks the table; I correct any awkward paraphrases; then commit. This catches bad translations cheaply.

4. **Branch:** stay on `feat/panorama-overview-mode`, consistent with how the user has been treating this branch as the rolling iteration line. If on review they'd rather isolate the data work, the commit can be cherry-picked to a new branch — but defaulting to the current branch keeps the workflow simple.

## Transformation rules

### Domain entry
- `key` = `slugify(english_domain_name)` (kebab-case, e.g., `AI R&D` → `ai-rd`)
- `label` = concise English translation
- `labelZh` = original Chinese as written
- `short` = same as `label` (the existing 5 domains use `short` for sidebar/breadcrumb abbreviations; if a translation is already short, reuse it)
- `pdts: PdtSeed[]` = list of PDT entries

### PDT entry
- `key` = composite `{domain-key}.{pdt-slug}` (matches existing convention; e.g., `ai-rd.dev-services`)
- `label` = concise English translation
- `labelZh` = original Chinese

### Tool entry (lives in `MCP_TOOLS`)
- `slug` = `slugify(english_tool_name)`; on collision across the whole `MCP_TOOLS` array, suffix with the PDT slug (e.g., `logging-dev-services`) — slugs must be globally unique.
- `name` = concise English translation
- `nameZh` = original Chinese
- `owner` = the PDT composite key (`{domain-key}.{pdt-slug}`)
- `blurb` = `""` (schema is NOT NULL but empty string is allowed; user didn't provide blurbs)
- `blurbZh` = `""`
- `mcps` = MCP entries (empty array if the tool has no children → frontend renders a `none`-status placeholder client-side)

**Special rule — tools with no MCPs are renamed.** If a tool line has no MCP children, both `name` and `nameZh` are rewritten to a *similar but not identical* paraphrase before slug derivation. The intent is to keep the semantic domain readable while making the surface text intentionally generic — typical transformations:

- Replace proprietary/internal acronyms with generic equivalents (e.g., `IPD` → `R&D`).
- Drop overly specific suffixes (`服务`, `系统`, `平台`, "Service", "System", "Platform") when they don't carry meaning.
- Keep the core technical/domain noun intact.

Example from the user: `IPD 质量管理服务` → `R&D 质量管理` (rename applied to `nameZh`; English translation flows from the renamed Chinese, e.g., `R&D Quality Management`). Slug is derived from the *renamed* English (`rd-quality-management`), not the original. Tools **with** MCPs keep their original naming verbatim.

Because this rule is judgment-based, I'll list every applied rename in the post-transform summary before running the seed so the user can correct any awkward paraphrases before they hit the DB.

### MCP entry
For a named `mcp <name>` line:
- `slug` = `slugify(english_mcp_name)`; on collision, suffix with the tool slug
- `name` = concise English translation
- `nameZh` = original Chinese
- `released: true`, `inDev: false`
- `depsCount: 0`, `tags: []`, `blurb: ""`, `blurbZh: ""`

For an `mcp_开发中` line:
- `slug` = `dev-{tool-slug}-{n}` where `n` is the 1-indexed position among the tool's `mcp_开发中` siblings
- `name` = `"In Development"`
- `nameZh` = `"开发中"`
- `released: false`, `inDev: true`
- `depsCount: 0`, `tags: []`, `blurb: ""`, `blurbZh: ""`

## Files to modify

1. **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts`** — fully rewrite the `PUBLIC_DOMAINS` array and replace all `MCP_TOOLS` entries whose `owner` is a public-layer key (i.e., contains a `.`). Keep `INDUSTRY_SECTORS` and all sector-owned tools (`owner` not containing `.`) untouched.

2. **`app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue`** — update the `BUCKET_FOR` constant (lines 34–38). With re-keyed domains, the existing entries (`airnd`, `prodsw`, `hardware`, `proddigi`, `infra`) become stale. The three buckets stay (`rnd`, `product`, `other`); I'll re-map each new domain key into the appropriate bucket. Specifically:
   - The user's "AI R&D" equivalent → `rnd`
   - The user's "Product & Software" equivalent → `product`
   - Every other new domain → `other`
   - Unknown keys still fall through to `other` (existing fallback), so the panel won't blank out if the user's tree disagrees with my mapping.

3. **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.test.ts:47`** — fixture asserts `ownerToParts("airnd.devsvcs")` returns `{ primary: "airnd", secondary: "devsvcs" }`. Replace the literal with whatever the new equivalent owner string is (e.g., `ai-rd.dev-services`). The assertion stays structurally identical.

4. **`app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.nuxt.test.ts:18`** — mock factory sets `ownerPrimary: "airnd", ownerSecondary: "devsvcs"`. Replace with the new equivalent. The test only cares that the owner fields exist, not their literal value.

## Seed pipeline run

After the file rewrite:

```bash
bun run db:seed:mcp
```

The seed script (`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`) is idempotent upsert-by-key with a final prune step. Expected behavior:
- Old domain rows whose `key` no longer appears in the new `PUBLIC_DOMAINS` → pruned by FK cascade or by the explicit prune in the script.
- Old tool rows whose `slug` is no longer in `MCP_TOOLS` → pruned.
- Old marketplace extension stubs (`mcp-{slug}`) tied to dropped MCPs → pruned.
- Industry sectors / tools → unchanged.

Requires `DATABASE_URL` env. If I can't reach the dev DB from here, I'll prepare the file rewrite and ask the user to run the seed locally.

## Verification

1. `bun run lint` — clean.
2. `bun run typecheck` — exit 0.
3. `bun run test` — 256/256 (after fixture updates in step 3 and 4 above).
4. **Seed run** — `bun run db:seed:mcp` exits 0; spot-check stdout for the expected counts (N domains inserted, M tools, K MCPs, P pruned).
5. **Manual browser check (user-side):** `bun run dev`, then:
   - `/zh/mcp/panorama` (overview, public, zh locale) — confirm the new Chinese domain titles appear in the three super-buckets; PDT sub-blocks reflect the new structure; each tool's MCPs render as pills.
   - `/en/mcp/panorama` — same, in concise English.
   - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` — confirm those modes render the new taxonomy correctly (they share the same `LayerPayload`).
   - Click any in-dev (`开发中`) pill → detail panel opens, shows `inDev: true` state.
   - Click any released pill → detail panel opens with link to the marketplace stub.

## Branch & commit flow

- **New branch.** This data re-import is logically separate from the Overview-view feature PR (#39, `feat/panorama-overview-mode`). It rewrites the entire public taxonomy, affecting the regular Panorama and List views too — not just the new Overview component. Recommend a fresh branch `chore/reseed-public-mcp-landscape` off `main` so PR #39 can ship as a pure UI feature without being held up by data-review.
- **If user prefers to bundle on the current branch:** I'll do that, but flag in the commit message that this is a data refresh, not an Overview tweak.
- **Single commit** for the rewrite + 3 downstream updates (`OverviewView.vue` BUCKET_FOR, two test fixtures) since they must ship together to keep the build green. Conventional commit: `chore(mcp-landscape): re-import public-layer taxonomy from new source-of-truth` (or `feat(...)` if the user prefers — purely a labeling call).
- **Seed run is a separate step** the user invokes against their DB; the commit only contains the source-of-truth file + downstream patches.
- No `Co-Authored-By:` trailer.
- Do not push or open a PR until the user explicitly says so.

## Critical files

- `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` — rewrite (public-layer entries)
- `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` — update `BUCKET_FOR` map
- `shared/data/mcp-landscape.test.ts` — fixture refresh
- `app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.nuxt.test.ts` — fixture refresh
- Reference only: `scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts` (upsert + prune behavior), `shared/db/schema/mcp-landscape.ts` (column constraints)

## What happens next

1. User approves this plan.
2. User pastes the hierarchical text tree (one message, full payload).
3. I parse it, apply the transformation rules, propose the translated English labels back to the user for any non-obvious term (e.g., domain-level names where translation choice matters for the URL slug) — corrections cheap before the seed run.
4. I rewrite `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` and the 3 downstream files.
5. Validate locally (lint, typecheck, test).
6. Commit. User runs the seed against their DB.

163	
164	function toSlug(name: string): string {
165	  return name
166	    .toLowerCase()
167	    .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "-")
168	    .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, "")
169	}
170	
171	function tool(
172	  name: string,
173	  owner: string,
174	  blurb: string,
175	  blurbZh: string,
176	  mcps: McpSeed[],
177	  opts: { nameZh?: string } = {},
178	): McpToolSeed {
179	  return {
180	    slug: toSlug(name),
181	    name,
182	    nameZh: opts.nameZh,
183	    owner,
184	    blurb,
185	    blurbZh,
186	    mcps,
187	  }
188	}
189	
190	function mcp(
191	  slug: string,
192	  status: McpStatus,
193	  depsCount: number,
194	  blurb: string,
195	  blurbZh: string,
196	  tags: string[],
197	  opts: { name?: string; nameZh?: string } = {},
198	): McpSeed {
199	  return {
200	    slug,
201	    name: opts.name,
202	    nameZh: opts.nameZh,
203	    released: status === "released",
204	    inDev: status === "dev",
205	    depsCount,
206	    blurb,
207	    blurbZh,
208	    tags,
209	  }
210	}

212	/** Convenience: a tool with a single MCP, slug explicit so it can describe
213	 * what the MCP exposes (`bgp-policy-mcp`, not `routeforge-mcp`). The MCP
214	 * inherits the tool's blurb by default. Multi-MCP tools call `tool(...)`
215	 * with an explicit mcps[] instead. */
216	function oneMcpTool(
217	  name: string,
218	  owner: string,
219	  blurb: string,
220	  blurbZh: string,
221	  mcpSlug: string,
222	  status: McpStatus,
223	  depsCount: number,
224	  tags: string[],
225	  opts: { nameZh?: string; mcpBlurb?: string; mcpBlurbZh?: string } = {},
226	): McpToolSeed {
227	  return tool(name, owner, blurb, blurbZh, [
228	    mcp(
229	      mcpSlug,
230	      status,
231	      depsCount,
232	      opts.mcpBlurb ?? blurb,
233	      opts.mcpBlurbZh ?? blurbZh,
234	      tags,
235	    ),
236	  ], { nameZh: opts.nameZh })
237	}
238	
239	/** Convenience: a tool with **no** MCPs yet — renders a single placeholder
240	 * tile. Used for legacy / manual-workflow tools that truly don't need one. */
241	function noMcpTool(
242	  name: string,
243	  owner: string,
244	  blurb: string,
245	  blurbZh: string,
246	  opts: { nameZh?: string } = {},
247	): McpToolSeed {
248	  return tool(name, owner, blurb, blurbZh, [], opts)
249	}

489	// ─── Helpers (used by both server and client) ────────────────────────────────
490	
491	/** Derive the runtime status of an MCP row. */
492	export function deriveStatus(row: {
493	  extensionId: string | null
494	  inDev: boolean
495	}): McpStatus {
496	  if (row.extensionId) return "released"
497	  if (row.inDev) return "dev"
498	  return "none"
499	}
500	
501	/** Roll a tool's MCPs up to a single tool-level status — "released wins". */
502	export function rollupStatus(statuses: McpStatus[]): McpStatus {
503	  if (statuses.some((s) => s === "released")) return "released"
504	  if (statuses.some((s) => s === "dev")) return "dev"
505	  return "none"
506	}
507	
508	/** Resolve the layer + group keys for a tool's `owner` path. */
509	export function ownerToParts(owner: string): {
510	  layer: "industry" | "public"
511	  primary: string
512	  secondary?: string
513	} {
514	  if (owner.includes(".")) {
515	    const [primary, secondary] = owner.split(".")
516	    return { layer: "public", primary: primary!, secondary }
517	  }
518	  return { layer: "industry", primary: owner }
519	}
