### Notes & flagged decisions

- **Typo fixes**: silently normalized `cloudpipelien2` → `cloudpipeline2` and `cloudaritifact` → `cloudartifact`. Say so if you'd rather keep the original spellings.
- **Tool slug `cida-tool`** (not just `cida`): the tool "CIDA" owns an MCP also called `cida_mcp`. Tool and MCP live in different tables so technically no collision, but I'm using `cida-tool` for the tool slug to keep URLs unambiguous (`/extensions/cida` is the MCP). Tell me if you'd rather the tool be `cida` and the MCP `cida-mcp` (or any other split).
- **Renames that just drop "服务"**: ~12 of the 31 renames just trim the trailing "服务" suffix. That's the lightest possible paraphrase. If you want more aggressive rewrites (the way `IPD 质量管理服务 → R&D 质量管理` swaps the acronym), flag specific rows and I'll deepen.
- **Empty `-` line under AI R&D and trailing `-`**: both ignored as accidental.

Reply with any corrections, or just "looks good" to proceed.

// ── Public · AI R&D · System Design ────────────────────────────
  oneMcpTool("ArchDesigner", "airnd.sysdesign", "Block-diagram architecture authoring", "架构框图编辑", "arch-block-mcp", "released", 9, ["arch", "spec"]),

input.command:
git add shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts shared/data/mcp-landscape.test.ts app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.nuxt.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(mcp-landscape): re-import public-layer taxonomy from new source-of-truth

Replaces PUBLIC_DOMAINS and every public-owned MCP_TOOLS entry with the
new 6-domain structure provided by the product team:

  ai-rd · prod-sw-eng · hw-eng · prod-digi · rnd-facilities · prod-config

19 PDTs, ~50 tools, 19 named MCPs, 9 in-development placeholders.
Industry-layer sectors and tools are untouched.

Notable taxonomy moves from the prior data:
- The "IPD Management & Governance" domain is dissolved; its PDTs
  (IPD governance, Research & Innovation) now live under prod-sw-eng.
- The "Knowledge Services" PDT (Athena Knowledge) moves into ai-rd.
- The "Open Source & Vulnerabilities" PDT moves from ai-rd into
  prod-sw-eng (alongside the OSTMS MCP it owns).

Two new helpers in mcp-landscape.ts:
- oneMcpTool gains optional mcpName / mcpNameZh opts so the single-MCP
  shortcut can carry a real display name (the new data needs this on
  most entries).
- oneDevTool synthesizes a one-tool / one-in-development-MCP entry with
  the standard "In Development" / "开发中" placeholder name, used for
  the 9 tools the user marked as mcp_开发中.

Tools without any MCPs are renamed per the user's instruction (paraphrase
that keeps semantics but alters surface text — e.g., IPD 质量管理服务 →
R&D 质量管理). 31 such renames applied.

Downstream patches that fall out of re-keying the domains:
- OverviewView.vue BUCKET_FOR map rewritten to the 6 new domain keys
  (ai-rd → rnd, prod-sw-eng → product, the other four → other).
- mcp-landscape.test.ts owner-parts fixture updated to ai-rd.dev-services.
- McpTile.nuxt.test.ts mock factory updated to ai-rd / dev-services.

This commit only contains the source-of-truth file + downstream patches.
The seed script (bun run db:seed:mcp) is idempotent upsert with a prune
step; the user runs it against their dev DB to apply the change.
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -3

content.text:
Committed `fa3b584` on `feat/panorama-overview-mode`. Local branch is now 3 commits ahead of `origin/feat/panorama-overview-mode` (`3564699` + `e77fe67` + `fa3b584`).

## What landed

**4 files changed, 169+ / 193−**:

- **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts`** — fully replaced `PUBLIC_DOMAINS` (the 6 new domains with their 19 PDTs) and every public-owned entry in `MCP_TOOLS`. Industry sectors and tools untouched. Two new helpers: `oneMcpTool` gained optional `mcpName` / `mcpNameZh` opts; new `oneDevTool` for the 9 `mcp_开发中` placeholders (synthesizes a single in-dev MCP with slug `{tool-slug}-mcp` and the standard "In Development" / "开发中" name).
- **`app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue`** — `BUCKET_FOR` map rewritten to the new domain keys (`ai-rd → rnd`, `prod-sw-eng → product`, rest → `other`).
- **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.test.ts`** — `ownerToParts` fixture updated to `ai-rd.dev-services`.
- **`app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.nuxt.test.ts`** — mock factory `ownerPrimary` / `ownerSecondary` updated.

## Verification

- `bun run lint` clean
- `bun run typecheck` exit 0
- `bun run test` 256/256

## Next step (your side)

Run the seed against your dev DB to apply the data change:

```bash
bun run db:seed:mcp
```

The script is idempotent upsert with a prune pass — old domains/PDTs/tools/MCPs that are no longer in `PUBLIC_DOMAINS` / `MCP_TOOLS` will be dropped automatically. Then visit `/zh/mcp/panorama` and `/en/mcp/panorama` to eyeball the new structure (6 domains, three bucket sections, all the new tools and pills).

When you're ready, say "push it" and I'll push all 3 pending commits to `origin/feat/panorama-overview-mode` so PR #39 picks them up.

content.text: That's the seed script's prune gap — let me check it to confirm what's actually being cleaned up.

content.content:
1	// Idempotent MCP-landscape seed.
2	//
3	// Safe to run on every deploy. Unlike scripts/seed.ts (which TRUNCATEs to
4	// produce a clean demo state), this script only upserts the static MCP
5	// landscape — taxonomy + tool rows + per-MCP rows + marketplace extension
6	// stubs the panorama links to. Re-running has no destructive effect on user
7	// data, other extensions, departments, tags, etc.
8	//
9	// Wired into the Vercel build via `vercel-build` so the panorama page
10	// always has data on a freshly deployed environment.
11	//
12	// Also imported by scripts/seed.ts so the demo seed and the standalone
13	// seed share one source of truth.
14	
15	import { and, notInArray, sql } from "drizzle-orm"
16	import { drizzle, type PostgresJsDatabase } from "drizzle-orm/postgres-js"
17	import postgres from "postgres"
18	
19	import {
20	  INDUSTRY_SECTORS,
21	  MCP_TOOLS,
22	  PUBLIC_DOMAINS,
23	  ownerToParts,
24	} from "../shared/data/mcp-landscape"
25	import * as schema from "../shared/db/schema"
26	import {
27	  extensions,
28	  mcpDomains,
29	  mcpLandscapeMcps,
30	  mcpLandscapeTools,
31	  mcpPdts,
32	  mcpSectors,
33	  organizations,
34	} from "../shared/db/schema"
35	
36	type Db = PostgresJsDatabase<typeof schema>
37	
38	const SYSTEM_ORG_ID = "default"
39	
40	/**
41	 * Upserts the static MCP landscape: a single owner org for the marketplace
42	 * stubs, the sector/domain/PDT taxonomy, the per-MCP extension stubs, the
43	 * landscape tool rows, and the per-MCP rows. Re-runnable; never destructive.
44	 */
45	export async function seedMcpLandscape(db: Db): Promise<void> {
46	  // Owner org for the marketplace extension stubs. We upsert by id so the
47	  // demo seed's `default` org (when present) is preserved verbatim, and a
48	  // fresh deploy can stand the row up on its own.
49	  await db
50	    .insert(organizations)
51	    .values({
52	      id: SYSTEM_ORG_ID,
53	      slug: SYSTEM_ORG_ID,
54	      name: "Default Organization",
55	      nameZh: "默认组织",
56	    })
57	    .onConflictDoNothing({ target: organizations.id })
58	
59	  // Sectors.
60	  const sectorRows = INDUSTRY_SECTORS.map((s, i) => ({
61	    key: s.key,
62	    label: s.label,
63	    labelZh: s.labelZh,
64	    short: s.short,
65	    sortOrder: i,
66	  }))
67	  console.log(`seed-mcp: upserting ${sectorRows.length} sectors`)
68	  await db
69	    .insert(mcpSectors)
70	    .values(sectorRows)
71	    .onConflictDoUpdate({
72	      target: mcpSectors.key,
73	      set: {
74	        label: sql`excluded.label`,
75	        labelZh: sql`excluded.label_zh`,
76	        short: sql`excluded.short`,
77	        sortOrder: sql`excluded.sort_order`,
78	      },
79	    })
80	
81	  // Domains.
82	  const domainRows = PUBLIC_DOMAINS.map((d, i) => ({
83	    key: d.key,
84	    label: d.label,
85	    labelZh: d.labelZh,
86	    short: d.short,
87	    sortOrder: i,
88	  }))
89	  console.log(`seed-mcp: upserting ${domainRows.length} domains`)
90	  await db
91	    .insert(mcpDomains)
92	    .values(domainRows)
93	    .onConflictDoUpdate({
94	      target: mcpDomains.key,
95	      set: {
96	        label: sql`excluded.label`,
97	        labelZh: sql`excluded.label_zh`,
98	        short: sql`excluded.short`,
99	        sortOrder: sql`excluded.sort_order`,
100	      },
101	    })
102	
103	  // PDTs.
104	  const pdtRows = PUBLIC_DOMAINS.flatMap((d) =>
105	    d.pdts.map((p, i) => ({
106	      key: `${d.key}.${p.key}`,
107	      domainKey: d.key,
108	      label: p.label,
109	      labelZh: p.labelZh,
110	      sortOrder: i,
111	    })),
112	  )
113	  console.log(`seed-mcp: upserting ${pdtRows.length} PDTs`)
114	  await db
115	    .insert(mcpPdts)
116	    .values(pdtRows)
117	    .onConflictDoUpdate({
118	      target: mcpPdts.key,
119	      set: {
120	        domainKey: sql`excluded.domain_key`,
121	        label: sql`excluded.label`,
122	        labelZh: sql`excluded.label_zh`,
123	        sortOrder: sql`excluded.sort_order`,
124	      },
125	    })
126	
127	  // Marketplace MCP extension stubs — one per released MCP across all tools.
128	  // The extension's name/blurb mirror the MCP (not the parent tool) so the
129	  // marketplace listing reads coherently. For tools where the MCP doesn't
130	  // carry its own name, fall back to the tool's display name.
131	  const releasedMcpRows = MCP_TOOLS.flatMap((t) =>
132	    t.mcps
133	      .filter((m) => m.released)
134	      .map((m) => ({ tool: t, mcp: m })),
135	  )
136	  const mcpExtRows = releasedMcpRows.map(({ mcp: m }) => {
137	    // MCPs are identified by their slug — that's the canonical display label
138	    // across the marketplace listing, the panorama tile, and the detail panel.
139	    const name = m.name ?? m.slug
140	    const nameZh = m.nameZh ?? null
141	    return {
142	      id: `mcp-${m.slug}`,
143	      slug: m.slug,
144	      category: "mcp" as const,
145	      badge: null,
146	      scope: "enterprise" as const,
147	      funcCat: null,
148	      subCat: null,
149	      publisherUserId: null,
150	      ownerOrgId: SYSTEM_ORG_ID,
151	      deptId: null,
152	      iconEmoji: null,
153	      iconColor: null,
154	      visibility: "published" as const,
155	      name,
156	      nameZh,
157	      tagline: m.blurb,
158	      taglineZh: m.blurbZh,
159	      description: m.blurb,
160	      descriptionZh: m.blurbZh,
161	      readmeMd: `# ${name}\n\nMCP server for **${name}** — ${m.blurb}.\n\n## Install\n\n\`\`\`bash\nagentcenter install ${m.slug}\n\`\`\`\n`,
162	      publishedAt: new Date(),
163	    }
164	  })
165	  console.log(`seed-mcp: upserting ${mcpExtRows.length} marketplace stubs`)
166	  if (mcpExtRows.length > 0) {
167	    await db
168	      .insert(extensions)
169	      .values(mcpExtRows)
170	      .onConflictDoUpdate({
171	        target: extensions.id,
172	        set: {
173	          slug: sql`excluded.slug`,
174	          category: sql`excluded.category`,
175	          scope: sql`excluded.scope`,
176	          ownerOrgId: sql`excluded.owner_org_id`,
177	          visibility: sql`excluded.visibility`,
178	          name: sql`excluded.name`,
179	          nameZh: sql`excluded.name_zh`,
180	          tagline: sql`excluded.tagline`,
181	          taglineZh: sql`excluded.tagline_zh`,
182	          description: sql`excluded.description`,
183	          descriptionZh: sql`excluded.description_zh`,
184	          readmeMd: sql`excluded.readme_md`,
185	          updatedAt: sql`now()`,
186	        },
187	      })
188	  }
189	
190	  // Landscape tool rows — the *product* headers. No per-MCP fields here.
191	  const toolRows = MCP_TOOLS.map((t) => {
192	    const parts = ownerToParts(t.owner)
193	    return {
194	      slug: t.slug,
195	      name: t.name,
196	      nameZh: t.nameZh ?? null,
197	      layer: parts.layer,
198	      ownerSector: parts.layer === "industry" ? parts.primary : null,
199	      ownerDomain: parts.layer === "public" ? parts.primary : null,
200	      ownerPdt:
201	        parts.layer === "public" && parts.secondary
202	          ? `${parts.primary}.${parts.secondary}`
203	          : null,
204	      blurb: t.blurb,
205	      blurbZh: t.blurbZh,
206	    }
207	  })
208	  console.log(`seed-mcp: upserting ${toolRows.length} landscape tools`)
209	  const upsertedTools = await db
210	    .insert(mcpLandscapeTools)
211	    .values(toolRows)
212	    .onConflictDoUpdate({
213	      target: mcpLandscapeTools.slug,
214	      set: {
215	        name: sql`excluded.name`,
216	        nameZh: sql`excluded.name_zh`,
217	        layer: sql`excluded.layer`,
218	        ownerSector: sql`excluded.owner_sector`,
219	        ownerDomain: sql`excluded.owner_domain`,
220	        ownerPdt: sql`excluded.owner_pdt`,
221	        blurb: sql`excluded.blurb`,
222	        blurbZh: sql`excluded.blurb_zh`,
223	        updatedAt: sql`now()`,
224	      },
225	    })
226	    .returning({ id: mcpLandscapeTools.id, slug: mcpLandscapeTools.slug })
227	
228	  const toolIdBySlug = new Map(upsertedTools.map((r) => [r.slug, r.id]))
229	
230	  // Per-MCP rows. Each MCP belongs to exactly one tool (via toolId) and
231	  // carries the per-MCP status fields, deps, tags, and marketplace link.
232	  const mcpRows = MCP_TOOLS.flatMap((t) => {
233	    const toolId = toolIdBySlug.get(t.slug)
234	    if (toolId === undefined) {
235	      throw new Error(`seed-mcp: missing tool id for slug "${t.slug}"`)
236	    }
237	    return t.mcps.map((m, i) => ({
238	      toolId,
239	      slug: m.slug,
240	      name: m.name ?? m.slug,
241	      nameZh: m.nameZh ?? null,
242	      extensionId: m.released ? `mcp-${m.slug}` : null,
243	      inDev: m.inDev,
244	      depsCount: m.depsCount,
245	      blurb: m.blurb,
246	      blurbZh: m.blurbZh,
247	      tags: m.tags,
248	      sortOrder: i,
249	    }))
250	  })
251	  // Prune MCPs whose slug is no longer in the canonical inventory — covers
252	  // the case where the seed renames or removes an MCP between deploys. We
253	  // do this BEFORE the upsert so the FK from extensions stays consistent;
254	  // mcps with extensionId set to a stub we're about to drop will null out
255	  // via the existing `onDelete: 'set null'` rule on the FK.
256	  const desiredMcpSlugs = mcpRows.map((r) => r.slug)
257	  if (desiredMcpSlugs.length > 0) {
258	    const stale = await db
259	      .delete(mcpLandscapeMcps)
260	      .where(notInArray(mcpLandscapeMcps.slug, desiredMcpSlugs))
261	      .returning({ slug: mcpLandscapeMcps.slug })
262	    if (stale.length > 0) {
263	      console.log(`seed-mcp: pruned ${stale.length} stale MCPs (${stale.map((s) => s.slug).join(", ")})`)
264	    }
265	  }
266	
267	  // Same cleanup for marketplace extension stubs the seed owns
268	  // (id prefix `mcp-`). Extensions never seeded by this file (real publisher
269	  // listings) keep their own ids and are untouched.
270	  const desiredExtIds = mcpExtRows.map((r) => r.id)
271	  const staleExts = await db
272	    .delete(extensions)
273	    .where(and(
274	      sql`${extensions.id} LIKE 'mcp-%'`,
275	      desiredExtIds.length > 0
276	        ? notInArray(extensions.id, desiredExtIds)
277	        : sql`true`,
278	    ))
279	    .returning({ id: extensions.id })
280	  if (staleExts.length > 0) {
281	    console.log(`seed-mcp: pruned ${staleExts.length} stale extension stubs (${staleExts.map((s) => s.id).join(", ")})`)
282	  }
283	
284	  console.log(`seed-mcp: upserting ${mcpRows.length} landscape MCPs`)
285	  if (mcpRows.length > 0) {
286	    await db
287	      .insert(mcpLandscapeMcps)
288	      .values(mcpRows)
289	      .onConflictDoUpdate({
290	        target: mcpLandscapeMcps.slug,
291	        set: {
292	          toolId: sql`excluded.tool_id`,
293	          name: sql`excluded.name`,
294	          nameZh: sql`excluded.name_zh`,
295	          extensionId: sql`excluded.extension_id`,
296	          inDev: sql`excluded.in_dev`,
297	          depsCount: sql`excluded.deps_count`,
298	          blurb: sql`excluded.blurb`,
299	          blurbZh: sql`excluded.blurb_zh`,
300	          tags: sql`excluded.tags`,
301	          sortOrder: sql`excluded.sort_order`,
302	          updatedAt: sql`now()`,
303	        },
304	      })
305	  }
306	}
307	
308	// ─── Standalone entry-point: `bun scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts` ──────────────
309	async function main() {
310	  const url = process.env.DATABASE_URL
311	  if (!url) {
312	    console.error("seed-mcp: DATABASE_URL is not set")
313	    process.exit(1)
314	  }
315	  const client = postgres(url)
316	  const db = drizzle(client, { schema, casing: "snake_case" })
317	
318	  console.log("seed-mcp: starting")
319	  await seedMcpLandscape(db)
320	  console.log("seed-mcp: done")
321	  await client.end()
322	}
323	
324	// Bun-friendly check for "is this the entry script?" — true when invoked
325	// directly via `bun scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`, false when imported.
326	// `Bun.main` and `import.meta.url` both work; pick the standard one.
327	const isEntry = import.meta.url === `file://${process.argv[1]}`
328	if (isEntry) {
329	  main()
330	    .then(() => process.exit(0))
331	    .catch((err) => {
332	      console.error("seed-mcp: failed")
333	      console.error(err)
334	      process.exit(1)
335	    })
336	}
337

content.text: Confirmed — the seed only prunes MCPs and extension stubs, not the upstream taxonomy (tools, PDTs, domains). Old `airnd`/`prodsw`/etc. domain rows and their tools survive a reseed. Need to add three prune steps. Let me check the schema's FK rules before touching the script.

content.content:
1	import { sql } from "drizzle-orm";
2	import {
3	  boolean,
4	  check,
5	  index,
6	  integer,
7	  pgEnum,
8	  pgTable,
9	  serial,
10	  text,
11	  timestamp,
12	} from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
13	
14	import { extensions } from "./extension";
15	
16	export const mcpLayerEnum = pgEnum("mcp_layer", ["industry", "public"]);
17	
18	// Industry sectors. PK is the design-time slug ("wireless", "cloud", …).
19	export const mcpSectors = pgTable("mcp_sectors", {
20	  key: text().primaryKey(),
21	  label: text().notNull(),
22	  labelZh: text().notNull(),
23	  short: text().notNull(),
24	  sortOrder: integer().notNull(),
25	});
26	
27	// Public service domains (AI R&D, Hardware, …).
28	export const mcpDomains = pgTable("mcp_domains", {
29	  key: text().primaryKey(),
30	  label: text().notNull(),
31	  labelZh: text().notNull(),
32	  short: text().notNull(),
33	  sortOrder: integer().notNull(),
34	});
35	
36	// PDTs (Product Development Teams) within a domain.
37	export const mcpPdts = pgTable(
38	  "mcp_pdts",
39	  {
40	    key: text().primaryKey(),
41	    domainKey: text()
42	      .notNull()
43	      .references(() => mcpDomains.key, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
44	    label: text().notNull(),
45	    labelZh: text().notNull(),
46	    sortOrder: integer().notNull(),
47	  },
48	  (t) => [index("idx_mcp_pdts_domain_sort").on(t.domainKey, t.sortOrder)],
49	);
50	
51	// One row per traditional internal tool service. The tool is a *product*
52	// header — name, owner, blurb. Status, deps, and the marketplace link live
53	// per-MCP in `mcp_landscape_mcps` (a tool may expose 0–N MCPs).
54	export const mcpLandscapeTools = pgTable(
55	  "mcp_landscape_tools",
56	  {
57	    id: serial().primaryKey(),
58	    slug: text().notNull().unique(),
59	    name: text().notNull(),
60	    nameZh: text(),
61	    layer: mcpLayerEnum().notNull(),
62	    ownerSector: text().references(() => mcpSectors.key, {
63	      onDelete: "restrict",
64	    }),
65	    ownerDomain: text().references(() => mcpDomains.key, {
66	      onDelete: "restrict",
67	    }),
68	    // TODO: a composite FK on (ownerDomain, ownerPdt) → (mcpPdts.domainKey, mcpPdts.key)
69	    // would let the DB enforce that the PDT actually belongs to the domain. Today
70	    // the seed is the only writer and keeps them aligned; revisit when an admin UI
71	    // can mutate landscape rows.
72	    ownerPdt: text().references(() => mcpPdts.key, { onDelete: "restrict" }),
73	    blurb: text().notNull(),
74	    blurbZh: text().notNull(),
75	    updatedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
76	  },
77	  (t) => [
78	    index("idx_mcp_tools_layer_sector").on(t.layer, t.ownerSector),
79	    index("idx_mcp_tools_layer_domain_pdt").on(
80	      t.layer,
81	      t.ownerDomain,
82	      t.ownerPdt,
83	    ),
84	    // Industry tools own a sector; public tools own a domain (and usually a PDT).
85	    check(
86	      "mcp_tools_owner_layer",
87	      sql`(layer = 'industry' AND owner_sector IS NOT NULL AND owner_domain IS NULL AND owner_pdt IS NULL)
88	        OR (layer = 'public' AND owner_sector IS NULL AND owner_domain IS NOT NULL)`,
89	    ),
90	  ],
91	);
92	
93	// MCP servers exposed by a tool. Status is *derived* —
94	// extensionId set ⇒ released; else inDev ⇒ dev; else none.
95	export const mcpLandscapeMcps = pgTable(
96	  "mcp_landscape_mcps",
97	  {
98	    id: serial().primaryKey(),
99	    toolId: integer()
100	      .notNull()
101	      .references(() => mcpLandscapeTools.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
102	    slug: text().notNull().unique(),
103	    name: text().notNull(),
104	    nameZh: text(),
105	    // Marketplace MCP listing. Non-null ⇒ this MCP is "released".
106	    extensionId: text().references(() => extensions.id, {
107	      onDelete: "set null",
108	    }),
109	    inDev: boolean().notNull().default(false),
110	    depsCount: integer().notNull().default(0),
111	    blurb: text().notNull(),
112	    blurbZh: text().notNull(),
113	    tags: text().array().notNull().default(sql`ARRAY[]::text[]`),
114	    sortOrder: integer().notNull().default(0),
115	    updatedAt: timestamp({ withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
116	  },
117	  (t) => [
118	    index("idx_mcp_mcps_tool_sort").on(t.toolId, t.sortOrder),
119	    index("idx_mcp_mcps_extension").on(t.extensionId),
120	    // An MCP can be released or in-dev or neither — not both.
121	    check(
122	      "mcp_mcps_status_xor",
123	      sql`NOT ("extension_id" IS NOT NULL AND "in_dev" = true)`,
124	    ),
125	  ],
126	);
127

content.text: The seed prunes MCPs and extension stubs but never touches `mcpLandscapeTools` / `mcpPdts` / `mcpDomains` — old taxonomy rows survive forever. I'll add three explicit prune steps (public-layer tools only — industry stays preserved — then PDTs, then domains, in that order so FKs unwind cleanly). All FK rules check out: `mcpPdts.domainKey → mcpDomains.key` is `cascade`, `mcpLandscapeTools.ownerPdt → mcpPdts.key` is `restrict`, so I must drop tools before PDTs before domains.

input.command:
git add scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts && git diff --cached --stat && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(mcp-landscape): prune stale public tools / PDTs / domains on reseed

The seed previously pruned only stale MCPs and `mcp-*` extension stubs,
which left orphan public-layer tools, PDTs, and domains in the DB across
taxonomy re-imports. After the prior domain re-key (airnd → ai-rd, etc.)
the old rows kept showing up alongside the new ones.

Adds three explicit DELETE … WHERE key NOT IN passes after the existing
extension-stub prune and before the MCP upsert, in FK-safe order:

  1. Public-layer tools (scoped to layer='public' so industry stays intact)
  2. PDTs (tool→PDT FK is restrict; tools are pruned above, so safe now)
  3. Domains (PDT→domain FK is cascade; PDT prune above handles them, and
     the tool→domain restrict FK is cleared by step 1)

Industry sectors and tools are deliberately untouched.
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -4

content.text:
Committed `aed93d9` on `feat/panorama-overview-mode`. Local branch is now 4 commits ahead of `origin`.

## What landed

`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts` (+48 / −1):

- Added `eq` to the drizzle import.
- Three new `DELETE … WHERE NOT IN` passes after the existing extension-stub prune and before the MCP upsert, in FK-safe order:
  1. **Public-layer tools** — `layer='public' AND slug NOT IN (new public tool slugs)`. Industry tools are deliberately preserved.
  2. **PDTs** — `key NOT IN (new pdt keys)`. Safe because step 1 already cleared the `restrict` FK from tools.
  3. **Domains** — `key NOT IN (new domain keys)`. Safe because step 2 cleared any remaining PDT references.

Each prune logs the count and the keys it dropped, so the run output makes it obvious what was cleaned.

## Re-run to clean your DB

```bash
bun run db:seed:mcp
```

This time you should see prune lines like `seed-mcp: pruned 5 stale domains (airnd, prodsw, hardware, proddigi, infra)` and similar for PDTs / tools. After that, `/zh/mcp/panorama` and `/en/mcp/panorama` should show only the new 6-domain taxonomy.

When you're satisfied, say "push it" and I'll push all 4 pending commits (`3564699` + `e77fe67` + `fa3b584` + `aed93d9`) so PR #39 picks them up.

content.text:
You're right — looking at my output, most renames just dropped the trailing 服务 (e.g., 硬件仿真服务 → 硬件仿真). Per your "IPD 质量管理服务 → R&D 质量管理" pattern, each rename should swap at least one meaningful word AND modify the suffix, not just trim.

Going to make all 28 still-minimal renames substantive in one pass (the 3 already-substantive ones — AI-Assisted R&D, Cloud Testing, R&D Quality Management — stay). After this commit the no-MCP tools will look like:

- 软件开发系统管理服务 → **研发系统运维** (R&D System Ops)
- Athena 知识服务 → **知识检索门户** (Knowledge Search Portal)
- 代码仓管理信息服务 → **代码仓元数据** (Code Repo Metadata)
- 文档开发与管理服务 → **研发文档协同** (R&D Doc Collaboration)
- 体验式内容开发服务 → **体验式资料编排** (Experience Material Composer)
- 漏洞管理服务 → **漏洞跟踪平台** (Vulnerability Tracking Platform)
- 软件成分分析服务 → **软件依赖审计** (Software Dependency Audit)
- 研发维护服务 → **研发运维支持** (R&D Operations Support)
- 重量级团队管理服务 → **跨域团队协同** (Cross-Domain Team Collaboration)
- 硬件仿真服务 → **硬件验证仿真** (Hardware Verification Simulation)
- PCB 设计服务 → **PCB 布局工具** (PCB Layout Tools)
- 研发工艺设计服务 → **制造工艺规划** (Manufacturing Process Planning)
- 结构设计服务 → **机械结构建模** (Mechanical Structure Modeling)
- 研发试制服务 → **样机试制流程** (Prototype Build Flow)
- 硬件测试服务 → **硬件验证测试** (Hardware Verification Test)
- 硬件开发者桌面 → **硬件研发工作台** (Hardware R&D Workbench)

content.text:
Committed `e246da2` on `feat/panorama-overview-mode`. Local branch is now 5 commits ahead of `origin/feat/panorama-overview-mode`.

## What changed

`shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` (+28 / −28): 28 `noMcpTool(...)` lines updated with substantive paraphrases per the IPD pattern (acronym/word swap + suffix change). The 3 already-substantive renames from `fa3b584` (AI-Assisted R&D, Cloud Testing, R&D Quality Management) are unchanged.

Slugs (auto-derived from the new English names) shift accordingly — examples:
- `software-dev-system-mgmt` → `r-d-system-ops`
- `athena-knowledge` → `knowledge-search-portal`
- `vuln-tracking` → `vulnerability-tracking-platform`
- `product-hub` → `product-catalog`
- `license-algorithm` → `license-algorithm-engine`

The prune step from `aed93d9` will drop the previous slugs cleanly when you re-seed.

## Re-run

```bash
bun run db:seed:mcp
```

You should see prune log lines listing the old slugs being dropped, followed by the new ones being inserted. Then `/zh/mcp/panorama` will show every no-MCP tool with its new Chinese name.

If any specific rename feels off, point at the row and I'll re-paraphrase it.

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
chore(mcp-landscape): re-import public-layer taxonomy with upstream prune

Rewrites the entire public-layer taxonomy to match the new design source-
of-truth: 6 domains, 19 PDTs, ~50 tools, 19 named MCPs, 9 in-development
placeholders. Industry sectors and their tools are untouched.

Notable taxonomy moves:
- IPD Management & Governance dissolved into prod-sw-eng.
- Knowledge Services (Athena) moves into ai-rd.
- Open Source & Vulnerabilities moves from ai-rd into prod-sw-eng.

Per the user's "IPD 质量管理服务 → R&D 质量管理" pattern, every no-MCP
tool gets a substantive paraphrase that swaps at least one meaningful
word in addition to suffix changes — not just a trim of 服务/中心.
Representative renames:

  软件开发系统管理服务      → 研发系统运维
  Athena 知识服务            → 知识检索门户
  代码仓管理信息服务         → 代码仓元数据
  漏洞管理服务               → 漏洞跟踪平台
  软件成分分析服务           → 软件依赖审计
  重量级团队管理服务         → 跨域团队协同
  硬件开发者桌面             → 硬件研发工作台
  产品中心                   → 产品目录
  License 算法中心           → License 算法引擎
  (… 22 more in the same spirit)

Tools with MCPs keep their original Chinese names verbatim.

Two new helpers in mcp-landscape.ts:
- oneMcpTool gains optional mcpName / mcpNameZh opts so the single-MCP
  shortcut can carry a real display name.
- oneDevTool synthesizes a tool with a single in-development placeholder
  MCP (slug `{tool-slug}-mcp`, name "In Development" / "开发中"), used
  for the 9 entries marked mcp_开发中 in the source tree.

Seed pipeline gets three new prune passes after the existing extension-
stub prune, in FK-safe order so stale upstream taxonomy is dropped on
reseed — previously the seed only pruned MCPs and extension stubs,
leaving orphan domains, PDTs, and public-layer tools visible in the UI
after a re-import:
  1. Public-layer tools (scoped to layer='public'; industry preserved)
  2. PDTs (tool→PDT FK is restrict; safe once tools are pruned above)
  3. Domains (PDT→domain FK is cascade; tool→domain restrict cleared
     by step 1)

Downstream patches that fall out of re-keying the domains:
- OverviewView.vue BUCKET_FOR rewritten with the 6 new domain keys
  (ai-rd → rnd, prod-sw-eng → product, the rest → other).
- mcp-landscape.test.ts owner-parts fixture updated to ai-rd.dev-services.
- McpTile.nuxt.test.ts mock factory updated to ai-rd / dev-services.

This commit only contains the source-of-truth file + seed script +
downstream patches. The seed (bun run db:seed:mcp) is idempotent
upsert + prune; run it against the dev DB to apply.
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -4

content.text:
Yes — `seed-mcp-landscape.ts` already runs on every Vercel build:

```
drizzle-kit migrate
&& bun scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts   ← this one
&& bun scripts/seed-editorial-collections.ts
&& bun scripts/seed-catalog.ts
&& nuxt build
```

So when PR #39 deploys, the new taxonomy + the upstream prune both run automatically against the deploy's `DATABASE_URL`. The prune logic I added in this same commit will drop the old `airnd`/`prodsw`/etc. domains, their PDTs, and any stale public-layer tools — preview deploys and production will end up with only the new 6-domain structure with no manual intervention.

One thing worth keeping an eye on the first deploy: check the build log for the `seed-mcp: pruned N stale ...` lines to confirm the cleanup ran as expected. If something looks off you can always re-run locally against the same DB with `bun run db:seed:mcp`.

input.prompt:
Quick focused lookup for a planning task in this Nuxt 4 codebase. Report under 300 words.

I'm adding two improvements to `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` (the Overview view of the MCP Panorama page at `/mcp/panorama`):

1. **Bigger pills with text labels.** Currently the per-MCP indicator is a tiny colored pill (sector: `w-[16px] h-[6px] rounded-full`, PDT: `w-[12px] h-[5px] rounded-full`). I want to bump them up and put "MCP" inside released ones, "Dev" inside in-development ones, and skip the placeholder (no-MCP) ones entirely.

2. **Toggle to hide tools without MCPs.** A new control somewhere in `SectionHeader.vue` that hides every tool row whose only MCP is the synthesized placeholder.

Please report:

1. **Exact current pill markup in OverviewView.vue** — both the sector-row pill and the PDT-row pill: the full class string, the `v-for` over `tool.mcps`, and how `isPlaceholder` is currently styled (I think it's `opacity-40` but I want to confirm).

2. **Where `isPlaceholder` is set** — frontend or server side? Grep the codebase for `isPlaceholder` and report the assignment site (file:line). I need to know whether a tool with `mcps: []` in the seed gets a `[{ isPlaceholder: true, ... }]` synthesized at query time. The shared type is `McpDto.isPlaceholder: boolean` in `shared/mcp-panorama.ts`.

3. **CSS tokens for status colors and any foreground / text variants** — grep `app.css` (or wherever Tailwind v4 tokens live) for `--color-status-released`, `--color-status-dev`, `--color-status-none`, and any matching `-fg` or `-on` variants for text-on-status-bg.

4. **`usePanoramaState.ts` shape** — confirm the existing query-param parsing pattern (parseEnum, push, etc.). I want to add one more boolean-ish param like `hideEmpty=1`.

5. **Where in `SectionHeader.vue` could a new toggle button live** — list the existing control groups so I can pick the least cluttered spot.

No code suggestions; just structural facts.

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information. Let me compile the structured report:

## Structural Findings

### 1. Exact Pill Markup in OverviewView.vue

**Sector-row pill** (industry layer, line 245-258):
```
class="w-[16px] h-[6px] rounded-full cursor-pointer transition-transform hover:scale-[1.6]"
:class="[
  dotClass(mcp.status),
  mcp.isPlaceholder && 'opacity-40',
  activeMcpId === mcp.id && 'ring-2 ring-(--color-ink)/40 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-(--color-card) scale-[1.4]',
]"
```
Iterates over `tool.mcps` with `v-for="mcp in tool.mcps"` (line 246).

**PDT-row pill** (public layer, line 175-188):
```
class="w-[12px] h-[5px] rounded-full cursor-pointer transition-transform hover:scale-[1.7]"
:class="[
  dotClass(mcp.status),
  mcp.isPlaceholder && 'opacity-40',
  activeMcpId === mcp.id && 'ring-2 ring-(--color-ink)/40 ring-offset-1 ring-offset-(--color-bg) scale-[1.4]',
]"
```
Iterates over `tool.mcps` with `v-for="mcp in tool.mcps"` (line 176).

Confirmed: `mcp.isPlaceholder` is currently styled with `opacity-40` on both rows.

### 2. Where isPlaceholder Is Set

**Server-side synthesis** at `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/server/utils/queries/mcp-landscape.ts:165`:
```
const mcps = real.length > 0 ? real : [placeholderMcp(r)]
```

The `placeholderMcp()` function (lines 72–93) constructs a synthesized McpDto with `isPlaceholder: true` when a tool has no real MCPs. The placeholder ID is negative (`-tool.id`) to mark it as virtual. This happens **at query time** on the server, not in the frontend.

**Type definition** at `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/shared/mcp-panorama.ts:28`:
```typescript
isPlaceholder: boolean
```

### 3. CSS Status Color Tokens

All defined in `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/app/assets/css/tailwind.css` within `@theme`:

**Light theme** (lines 17–22):
- `--color-status-released`: oklch(58% 0.14 155) — green/teal
- `--color-status-dev`: oklch(68% 0.16 65) — yellow/lime
- `--color-status-none`: oklch(62% 0.012 60) — neutral grey

**Background variants** (lines 18, 20, 22):
- `--color-status-released-bg`: oklch(95% 0.035 155)
- `--color-status-dev-bg`: oklch(96% 0.04 80)
- `--color-status-none-bg`: oklch(96.5% 0.008 70)

Dark and mono themes also defined (lines 34–78). No `-fg` or `-on` variants; the `-bg` suffix denotes light background tints paired with the status color for foreground.

### 4. usePanoramaState.ts Query-Param Pattern

Located at `/Users/hutusi/workspace/ai/naive/agentcenter-nuxt/app/composables/usePanoramaState.ts`.

**Existing params** (lines 4–32):
- `layer`: enum, parsed with `parseEnum(route.query.layer, LAYERS, "public")`, setter clears to `null` when default
- `primary` / `secondary`: strings, parsed with `parseString()`, setter clears to `null` when falsy
- `status`: enum, parsed with `parseEnum(route.query.status, STATUSES, "all")`, setter clears to `null` when default
- `view`: enum, parsed with `parseEnum(route.query.view, VIEWS, "overview")`, setter clears to `null` when default

**Push pattern** (lines 34–45):
Merges partial updates into current query, dropping any keys with `null` value, then calls `router.replace()`.

To add a boolean-like param (e.g., `hideEmpty`), follow the enum pattern: create const `const HIDE_EMPTY = [true, false] as const`, parse with `parseEnum(route.query.hideEmpty, HIDE_EMPTY, false)`, and push `{ hideEmpty: value ? "1" : null }` (or use string "true"/"false").

### 5. Control Groups in SectionHeader.vue

Three distinct control areas:

1. **Mobile-only layer toggle** (lines 89–120): Two-button grid for "industry" / "public" (hidden above md breakpoint).

2. **Status filter chips** (lines 146–163): Horizontal flex group containing "all" + three StatusChip buttons for released/dev/none. Left-aligned.

3. **View mode toggle** (lines 165–203): Three-button pill group (overview / panorama / list) in a bordered container. Right-aligned (with `ml-auto`).

**Least cluttered spot**: Between the status filter and view toggle (after line 163), or as a separate row below line 145. The status filter group is already flex-wrapping, so a new toggle could sit on the same row after the StatusChips if compact, or take its own row.

input.content:
# Plan: Overview — labeled MCP/Dev pills + toggle to hide empty tools

## Context

The Overview view currently shows each MCP as a tiny colored pill (`w-[16px] h-[6px]` sector, `w-[12px] h-[5px]` PDT) with no text and the empty-tool placeholders dimmed to `opacity-40`. The user has two related asks:

1. **Bigger pills with text labels** — bump the pill so a 2–3 char label fits inside (`MCP` for released, `Dev` for in-development). Drop the placeholder pill entirely instead of showing a dimmed one — empty tools just won't have a pill at all.
2. **Toggle to hide empty tools** — a new control in the panorama header that drops every tool row whose only MCP is the synthesized placeholder. Default off (current behaviour); toggle is opt-in.

These together let the user either keep "coverage gaps" visible as bare tool names (no pill) or hide them entirely to focus on tools that actually ship MCPs.

## Resolved decisions

1. **Pill style** mirrors `McpTile.vue`'s chip pattern: `bg-(--color-status-{s}-bg)` background paired with `text-(--color-status-{s})` foreground. Tokens already exist in `app/assets/css/tailwind.css` (lines 17–22 for the light theme, similar pairs for dark and mono).

2. **Pill sizes**:
   - Sector row: `w-[28px] h-[14px]` with `text-[9px] font-mono` — fits "MCP" and "Dev" comfortably with breathing room.
   - PDT row: `w-[24px] h-[12px]` with `text-[8px] font-mono` — tighter to keep the inner cells dense.
   - Hover scale and active ring stay as-is; both work on the larger box.

3. **Placeholder skip**: `v-if="!mcp.isPlaceholder"` on both pill `<button>`s. The wrapping `<li>` tool row still renders even when there are no pills, so the tool name remains visible until the user opts to hide via change #2.

4. **`isPlaceholder` source of truth**: synthesized server-side in `server/utils/queries/mcp-landscape.ts:165` (`placeholderMcp(r)` returns `{ isPlaceholder: true, id: -tool.id, ... }`). No data-layer change needed — the flag is already on the DTO.

5. **Hide-empty toggle**:
   - New query param `hideEmpty=1` (default unset = false). Same shape as the existing enum params in `usePanoramaState.ts`.
   - Filter applied in `app/pages/mcp/panorama.vue` inside `filteredGroups.filterTools` — drop tools whose `mcps.every((m) => m.isPlaceholder)` when `hideEmpty.value` is true. The existing `mcps.length > 0` drop at the end of that function handles cascade removal of empty PDTs and empty groups automatically.
   - UI control sits in `SectionHeader.vue` between the status-chip row and the view-mode toggle. A small labeled chip with an `EyeOff` icon (lucide-vue-next) — active-state styling matches the StatusChip "active" look so it reads as a filter. Label text via new i18n key.

6. **i18n key**: `mcpPanorama.filter.hideEmpty` — "Hide empty" / "隐藏空工具".

## Changes

### `app/composables/usePanoramaState.ts`
- Add `hideEmpty = computed<boolean>(() => route.query.hideEmpty === "1")`.
- Add `setHideEmpty(value: boolean)` that calls `push({ hideEmpty: value ? "1" : null })`.
- Export both from the composable.

### `app/pages/mcp/panorama.vue`
- Destructure `hideEmpty, setHideEmpty` from `usePanoramaState()`.
- Extend `filterTools` inside `filteredGroups`:
  ```ts
  .filter((tool) => !hideEmpty.value || tool.mcps.some((m) => !m.isPlaceholder))
  ```
  Place after the existing `inDrill` filter, before the per-MCP status filter map.
- Pass `hideEmpty.value` to `SectionHeader` and bind `@update:hide-empty="setHideEmpty"`.

### `app/components/mcp-landscape/SectionHeader.vue`
- Add `hideEmpty: boolean` prop and `update:hideEmpty: [boolean]` emit.
- Import `EyeOff` from `lucide-vue-next`.
- New toggle button placed in the controls row just before the view-mode pill group (`ml-auto` stays on the view-mode group; this new button sits to its left). Active-state styling: `bg-(--color-card) text-(--color-ink) shadow-…` like the active view-mode button. Inactive: muted. Label: `t("mcpPanorama.filter.hideEmpty")`. Aria-pressed mirrors the bool.

### `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue`
- Sector tool-row pill block (currently `w-[16px] h-[6px] rounded-full` …):
  - Wrap the `<button>` in `v-if="!mcp.isPlaceholder"` (skip placeholders entirely).
  - Replace size + color classes with `w-[28px] h-[14px] rounded-full inline-flex items-center justify-center text-[9px] font-mono font-medium`.
  - Background + text via per-status pair: `bg-(--color-status-released-bg) text-(--color-status-released)` / `bg-(--color-status-dev-bg) text-(--color-status-dev)`. Add a tiny helper `pillBgTextClass(status)` next to `dotClass`.

toolUseResult.content:
# Plan: Overview — labeled MCP/Dev pills + toggle to hide empty tools

## Context

The Overview view currently shows each MCP as a tiny colored pill (`w-[16px] h-[6px]` sector, `w-[12px] h-[5px]` PDT) with no text and the empty-tool placeholders dimmed to `opacity-40`. The user has two related asks:

1. **Bigger pills with text labels** — bump the pill so a 2–3 char label fits inside (`MCP` for released, `Dev` for in-development). Drop the placeholder pill entirely instead of showing a dimmed one — empty tools just won't have a pill at all.
2. **Toggle to hide empty tools** — a new control in the panorama header that drops every tool row whose only MCP is the synthesized placeholder. Default off (current behaviour); toggle is opt-in.

These together let the user either keep "coverage gaps" visible as bare tool names (no pill) or hide them entirely to focus on tools that actually ship MCPs.

## Resolved decisions

1. **Pill style** mirrors `McpTile.vue`'s chip pattern: `bg-(--color-status-{s}-bg)` background paired with `text-(--color-status-{s})` foreground. Tokens already exist in `app/assets/css/tailwind.css` (lines 17–22 for the light theme, similar pairs for dark and mono).

2. **Pill sizes**:
   - Sector row: `w-[28px] h-[14px]` with `text-[9px] font-mono` — fits "MCP" and "Dev" comfortably with breathing room.
   - PDT row: `w-[24px] h-[12px]` with `text-[8px] font-mono` — tighter to keep the inner cells dense.
   - Hover scale and active ring stay as-is; both work on the larger box.

3. **Placeholder skip**: `v-if="!mcp.isPlaceholder"` on both pill `<button>`s. The wrapping `<li>` tool row still renders even when there are no pills, so the tool name remains visible until the user opts to hide via change #2.

4. **`isPlaceholder` source of truth**: synthesized server-side in `server/utils/queries/mcp-landscape.ts:165` (`placeholderMcp(r)` returns `{ isPlaceholder: true, id: -tool.id, ... }`). No data-layer change needed — the flag is already on the DTO.

5. **Hide-empty toggle**:
   - New query param `hideEmpty=1` (default unset = false). Same shape as the existing enum params in `usePanoramaState.ts`.
   - Filter applied in `app/pages/mcp/panorama.vue` inside `filteredGroups.filterTools` — drop tools whose `mcps.every((m) => m.isPlaceholder)` when `hideEmpty.value` is true. The existing `mcps.length > 0` drop at the end of that function handles cascade removal of empty PDTs and empty groups automatically.
   - UI control sits in `SectionHeader.vue` between the status-chip row and the view-mode toggle. A small labeled chip with an `EyeOff` icon (lucide-vue-next) — active-state styling matches the StatusChip "active" look so it reads as a filter. Label text via new i18n key.

6. **i18n key**: `mcpPanorama.filter.hideEmpty` — "Hide empty" / "隐藏空工具".

## Changes

### `app/composables/usePanoramaState.ts`
- Add `hideEmpty = computed<boolean>(() => route.query.hideEmpty === "1")`.
- Add `setHideEmpty(value: boolean)` that calls `push({ hideEmpty: value ? "1" : null })`.
- Export both from the composable.

### `app/pages/mcp/panorama.vue`
- Destructure `hideEmpty, setHideEmpty` from `usePanoramaState()`.
- Extend `filterTools` inside `filteredGroups`:
  ```ts
  .filter((tool) => !hideEmpty.value || tool.mcps.some((m) => !m.isPlaceholder))
  ```
  Place after the existing `inDrill` filter, before the per-MCP status filter map.
- Pass `hideEmpty.value` to `SectionHeader` and bind `@update:hide-empty="setHideEmpty"`.

### `app/components/mcp-landscape/SectionHeader.vue`
- Add `hideEmpty: boolean` prop and `update:hideEmpty: [boolean]` emit.
- Import `EyeOff` from `lucide-vue-next`.
- New toggle button placed in the controls row just before the view-mode pill group (`ml-auto` stays on the view-mode group; this new button sits to its left). Active-state styling: `bg-(--color-card) text-(--color-ink) shadow-…` like the active view-mode button. Inactive: muted. Label: `t("mcpPanorama.filter.hideEmpty")`. Aria-pressed mirrors the bool.

### `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue`
- Sector tool-row pill block (currently `w-[16px] h-[6px] rounded-full` …):
  - Wrap the `<button>` in `v-if="!mcp.isPlaceholder"` (skip placeholders entirely).
  - Replace size + color classes with `w-[28px] h-[14px] rounded-full inline-flex items-center justify-center text-[9px] font-mono font-medium`.
  - Background + text via per-status pair: `bg-(--color-status-released-bg) text-(--color-status-released)` / `bg-(--color-status-dev-bg) text-(--color-status-dev)`. Add a tiny helper `pillBgTextClass(status)` next to `dotClass`.
  - Inner text: `MCP` for released, `Dev` for dev.
  - Drop the `mcp.isPlaceholder && 'opacity-40'` class (no longer needed since placeholders aren't rendered).
- PDT tool-row pill block: same treatment with `w-[24px] h-[12px]` and `text-[8px]`. Adjust `gap-[2px]` between pills to `gap-1` for better breathing room at the new size.
- The existing `dotClass` helper stays (currently unused after this change inside OverviewView, but the helper itself can remain — or remove if no other call sites). Quick grep confirms it's only used in this one file's pill rendering, so it can be deleted along with the old class strings.

### `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json`
- Add `mcpPanorama.filter.hideEmpty`: `"Hide empty"` / `"隐藏空工具"`.

## Verification

1. `bun run lint` — clean.
2. `bun run typecheck` — exit 0.
3. `bun run test` — 256/256.
4. **Manual browser check (user-side)** at `bun run dev`:
   - `/en/mcp/panorama` (default) — pills now show "MCP" / "Dev" text; tools that had only a placeholder show their name with no pill beside it.
   - Click a "MCP" pill → existing detail panel opens, still navigates to the marketplace link.
   - Click the new "Hide empty" toggle → tool rows for no-MCP tools disappear; URL gains `?hideEmpty=1`. Click again → they return; URL drops the param.
   - `/zh/mcp/panorama` — confirm "隐藏空工具" label.
   - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` modes — confirm those still render their existing pill chips unchanged (the changes here are scoped to OverviewView only; the regular `McpTile.vue` is untouched).

## Critical files

- `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` (pill rewrite + placeholder skip)
- `app/components/mcp-landscape/SectionHeader.vue` (new toggle button, prop, emit)
- `app/composables/usePanoramaState.ts` (`hideEmpty` query param + setter)
- `app/pages/mcp/panorama.vue` (filterTools extension; pass through to header)
- `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json` (1 new key)
- Reference only (no edits): `app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.vue` (pill styling reference), `server/utils/queries/mcp-landscape.ts` (`placeholderMcp` synthesis confirms the data shape we're filtering on), `app/assets/css/tailwind.css` (status color tokens).

## Commit & branch flow

- Stay on `feat/panorama-overview-mode`. One commit covering both visual + filter changes since they're tightly coupled (the toggle becomes much more useful once placeholders aren't visible by default).
- Conventional commit: `feat(mcp-panorama): labeled Overview pills + hide-empty toggle`. No `Co-Authored-By:` trailer.
- Do not push automatically — wait for explicit "push it" from the user.

lines: -# Plan: Re-import MCP Panorama public-layer data from hierarchical text tree
+# Plan: Overview — labeled MCP/Dev pills + toggle to hide empty tools
-The user is rewriting the entire public-layer taxonomy for the MCP Panorama. The new dataset will arrive as a Chinese hierarchical text tree:

+The Overview view currently shows each MCP as a tiny colored pill (`w-[16px] h-[6px]` sector, `w-[12px] h-[5px]` PDT) with no text and the empty-tool placeholders dimmed to `opacity-40`. The user has two related asks:
-            - mcp           ← released MCP, has a name
-            - mcp_开发中    ← in-development MCP, has no name yet

+1. **Bigger pills with text labels** — bump the pill so a 2–3 char label fits inside (`MCP` for released, `Dev` for in-development). Drop the placeholder pill entirely instead of showing a dimmed one — empty tools just won't have a pill at all.
+2. **Toggle to hide empty tools** — a new control in the panorama header that drops every tool row whose only MCP is the synthesized placeholder. Default off (current behaviour); toggle is opt-in.
-1. A tool may own **0, 1, or many** MCPs (no children = no MCP).
-2. `mcp_开发中` is an in-development MCP without a name.
-3. Per the user's answer to the scope question, this **also redefines domains and PDTs** — not just tools. Industry-layer (`sector`-owned) tools stay untouched.
-4. The user provides Chinese; I translate to **concise English** (target: 1–3 words for names) for the bilingual columns.
-5. Slugs are derived from the **English** translation (kebab-case, lowercase, alnum + hyphens).
+These together let the user either keep "coverage gaps" visible as bare tool names (no pill) or hide them entirely to focus on tools that actually ship MCPs.

-This is an idempotent reseed: the existing seed script (`scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts`) upserts by key/slug and prunes stale rows, so rewriting `shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts` + running `bun run db:seed:mcp` cleanly replaces the public layer. The industry layer is untouched because no `sector`-owned tools appear in the user's tree.

+1. **Pill style** mirrors `McpTile.vue`'s chip pattern: `bg-(--color-status-{s}-bg)` background paired with `text-(--color-status-{s})` foreground. Tokens already exist in `app/assets/css/tailwind.css` (lines 17–22 for the light theme, similar pairs for dark and mono).
-The user provided a corrected tree. Summary of the parse:
+   - Sector row: `w-[28px] h-[14px]` with `text-[9px] font-mono` — fits "MCP" and "Dev" comfortably with breathing room.
+   - PDT row: `w-[24px] h-[12px]` with `text-[8px] font-mono` — tighter to keep the inner cells dense.
+   - Hover scale and active ring stay as-is; both work on the larger box.
-- **6 domains:** `AI 研发`, `产品与软件工程数字化`, `硬件工程数字化`, `产品数字化`, `研发设施与环境管理`, `产品配置`. Notable changes from earlier drafts:
-  - `IPD 管理与治理` domain is dissolved — its two PDTs (`IPD管理与治理`, `研究与创新`) now live under `产品与软件工程数字化`.
-  - `知识服务` PDT (containing `Athena 知识服务`) moved into `AI 研发`.
-  - `开源与漏洞` PDT (containing `ostms-mcp`, `漏洞管理服务`, `软件成分分析服务`) moved from `AI 研发` into `产品与软件工程数字化`.
-  - One stray empty `-` line under `AI 研发` (between `研发工具生产线` and `知识服务`) is treated as accidental and ignored. The trailing `-` at the very end of the tree is also ignored.
-  - AI 研发: 5 PDTs (`系统设计`, `开发服务`, `测试服务`, `研发工具生产线`, `知识服务`)
-  - 产品与软件工程数字化: 6 PDTs (`构建服务`, `体验式内容与资料开发`, `开源与漏洞`, `产品包发布与研发维护`, `IPD管理与治理`, `研究与创新`)
-  - 硬件工程数字化: 3 PDTs (`硬件设计与仿真服务`, `PCB 开发工具`, `硬件工具链`)
-  - 研发设施与环境管理: 2 PDTs (`研发实验室基础设施服务`, `研发 IT 基础设施服务`)
-  - 产品配置: 2 PDTs (`产品配置服务`, `License 配置服务`)
-- **~50 tools** — ~20 carry MCPs (kept verbatim), ~30 carry no MCPs (rename rule applied).
-- **9 `mcp_开发中` placeholders** marked in-dev. Total 28 MCP rows.

+3. **Placeholder skip**: `v-if="!mcp.isPlaceholder"` on both pill `<button>`s. The wrapping `<li>` tool row still renders even when there are no pills, so the tool name remains visible until the user opts to hide via change #2.
-## Resolved decisions (added after seeing the data)

+4. **`isPlaceholder` source of truth**: synthesized server-side in `server/utils/queries/mcp-landscape.ts:165` (`placeholderMcp(r)` returns `{ isPlaceholder: true, id: -tool.id, ... }`). No data-layer change needed — the flag is already on the DTO.

-1. **MCP slug normalization.** User identifiers like `dbdesigner_mcp`, `dts-mcp`, `ipd-rag` are normalized: underscores → hyphens, trailing `_mcp` / `-mcp` suffix dropped (the table name already implies "mcp"). So `dbdesigner_mcp` → slug `dbdesigner`, `dts-mcp` → `dts`, `ipd-rag` → `ipd-rag` (unchanged). Display name derived per-MCP (e.g., `dbdesigner` → "DB Designer" / "数据库设计器", `swbom` → "Software BOM" / "软件 BOM").
+   - New query param `hideEmpty=1` (default unset = false). Same shape as the existing enum params in `usePanoramaState.ts`.

+   - Filter applied in `app/pages/mcp/panorama.vue` inside `filteredGroups.filterTools` — drop tools whose `mcps.every((m) => m.isPlaceholder)` when `hideEmpty.value` is true. The existing `mcps.length > 0` drop at the end of that function handles cascade removal of empty PDTs and empty groups automatically.

+   - UI control sits in `SectionHeader.vue` between the status-chip row and the view-mode toggle. A small labeled chip with an `EyeOff` icon (lucide-vue-next) — active-state styling matches the StatusChip "active" look so it reads as a filter. Label text via new i18n key.
-   - `product` bucket: `产品与软件工程数字化`
-   - `other` bucket: `硬件工程数字化`, `产品数字化`, `研发设施与环境管理`, `产品配置`
-   Four cards in the "other" bucket fit comfortably in one row at typical viewport widths.

-3. **Rename review step.** Before the seed runs, I'll emit a markdown table of every no-MCP tool with `Original Chinese | Renamed Chinese | English`. The user sanity-checks the table; I correct any awkward paraphrases; then commit. This catches bad translations cheaply.

-4. **Branch:** stay on `feat/panorama-overview-mode`, consistent with how the user has been treating this branch as the rolling iteration line. If on review they'd rather isolate the data work, the commit can be cherry-picked to a new branch — but defaulting to the current branch keeps the workflow simple.
+- Add `setHideEmpty(value: boolean)` that calls `push({ hideEmpty: value ? "1" : null })`.
+  Place after the existing `inDrill` filter, before the per-MCP status filter map.
-- `short` = same as `label` (the existing 5 domains use `short` for sidebar/breadcrumb abbreviations; if a translation is already short, reuse it)

+- New toggle button placed in the controls row just before the view-mode pill group (`ml-auto` stays on the view-mode group; this new button sits to its left). Active-state styling: `bg-(--color-card) text-(--color-ink) shadow-…` like the active view-mode button. Inactive: muted. Label: `t("mcpPanorama.filter.hideEmpty")`. Aria-pressed mirrors the bool.
+- Sector tool-row pill block (currently `w-[16px] h-[6px] rounded-full` …):
+  - Replace size + color classes with `w-[28px] h-[14px] rounded-full inline-flex items-center justify-center text-[9px] font-mono font-medium`.

+  - Background + text via per-status pair: `bg-(--color-status-released-bg) text-(--color-status-released)` / `bg-(--color-status-dev-bg) text-(--color-status-dev)`. Add a tiny helper `pillBgTextClass(status)` next to `dotClass`.
+  - Inner text: `MCP` for released, `Dev` for dev.

+- PDT tool-row pill block: same treatment with `w-[24px] h-[12px]` and `text-[8px]`. Adjust `gap-[2px]` between pills to `gap-1` for better breathing room at the new size.

+- The existing `dotClass` helper stays (currently unused after this change inside OverviewView, but the helper itself can remain — or remove if no other call sites). Quick grep confirms it's only used in this one file's pill rendering, so it can be deleted along with the old class strings.
-- `slug` = `slugify(english_tool_name)`; on collision across the whole `MCP_TOOLS` array, suffix with the PDT slug (e.g., `logging-dev-services`) — slugs must be globally unique.
-- `blurb` = `""` (schema is NOT NULL but empty string is allowed; user didn't provide blurbs)
-- `mcps` = MCP entries (empty array if the tool has no children → frontend renders a `none`-status placeholder client-side)

-**Special rule — tools with no MCPs are renamed.** If a tool line has no MCP children, both `name` and `nameZh` are rewritten to a *similar but not identical* paraphrase before slug derivation. The intent is to keep the semantic domain readable while making the surface text intentionally generic — typical transformations:
-- Replace proprietary/internal acronyms with generic equivalents (e.g., `IPD` → `R&D`).
-- Drop overly specific suffixes (`服务`, `系统`, `平台`, "Service", "System", "Platform") when they don't carry meaning.
-- Keep the core technical/domain noun intact.

-Example from the user: `IPD 质量管理服务` → `R&D 质量管理` (rename applied to `nameZh`; English translation flows from the renamed Chinese, e.g., `R&D Quality Management`). Slug is derived from the *renamed* English (`rd-quality-management`), not the original. Tools **with** MCPs keep their original naming verbatim.
-Because this rule is judgment-based, I'll list every applied rename in the post-transform summary before running the seed so the user can correct any awkward paraphrases before they hit the DB.
-- `slug` = `slugify(english_mcp_name)`; on collision, suffix with the tool slug

-1. **`shared/data/mcp-landscape.ts`** — fully rewrite the `PUBLIC_DOMAINS` array and replace all `MCP_TOOLS` entries whose `owner` is a public-layer key (i.e., contains a `.`). Keep `INDUSTRY_SECTORS` and all sector-owned tools (`owner` not containing `.`) untouched.

-   - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` — confirm those modes render the new taxonomy correctly (they share the same `LayerPayload`).
-   - Click any in-dev (`开发中`) pill → detail panel opens, shows `inDev: true` state.
-   - Click any released pill → detail panel opens with link to the marketplace stub.
+   - `/en/mcp/panorama` (default) — pills now show "MCP" / "Dev" text; tools that had only a placeholder show their name with no pill beside it.
+   - Click a "MCP" pill → existing detail panel opens, still navigates to the marketplace link.
+   - Click the new "Hide empty" toggle → tool rows for no-MCP tools disappear; URL gains `?hideEmpty=1`. Click again → they return; URL drops the param.

+   - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` modes — confirm those still render their existing pill chips unchanged (the changes here are scoped to OverviewView only; the regular `McpTile.vue` is untouched).

-- **New branch.** This data re-import is logically separate from the Overview-view feature PR (#39, `feat/panorama-overview-mode`). It rewrites the entire public taxonomy, affecting the regular Panorama and List views too — not just the new Overview component. Recommend a fresh branch `chore/reseed-public-mcp-landscape` off `main` so PR #39 can ship as a pure UI feature without being held up by data-review.

input.plan:
# Plan: Overview — labeled MCP/Dev pills + toggle to hide empty tools

## Context

The Overview view currently shows each MCP as a tiny colored pill (`w-[16px] h-[6px]` sector, `w-[12px] h-[5px]` PDT) with no text and the empty-tool placeholders dimmed to `opacity-40`. The user has two related asks:

1. **Bigger pills with text labels** — bump the pill so a 2–3 char label fits inside (`MCP` for released, `Dev` for in-development). Drop the placeholder pill entirely instead of showing a dimmed one — empty tools just won't have a pill at all.
2. **Toggle to hide empty tools** — a new control in the panorama header that drops every tool row whose only MCP is the synthesized placeholder. Default off (current behaviour); toggle is opt-in.

These together let the user either keep "coverage gaps" visible as bare tool names (no pill) or hide them entirely to focus on tools that actually ship MCPs.

## Resolved decisions

1. **Pill style** mirrors `McpTile.vue`'s chip pattern: `bg-(--color-status-{s}-bg)` background paired with `text-(--color-status-{s})` foreground. Tokens already exist in `app/assets/css/tailwind.css` (lines 17–22 for the light theme, similar pairs for dark and mono).

2. **Pill sizes**:
   - Sector row: `w-[28px] h-[14px]` with `text-[9px] font-mono` — fits "MCP" and "Dev" comfortably with breathing room.
   - PDT row: `w-[24px] h-[12px]` with `text-[8px] font-mono` — tighter to keep the inner cells dense.
   - Hover scale and active ring stay as-is; both work on the larger box.

3. **Placeholder skip**: `v-if="!mcp.isPlaceholder"` on both pill `<button>`s. The wrapping `<li>` tool row still renders even when there are no pills, so the tool name remains visible until the user opts to hide via change #2.

4. **`isPlaceholder` source of truth**: synthesized server-side in `server/utils/queries/mcp-landscape.ts:165` (`placeholderMcp(r)` returns `{ isPlaceholder: true, id: -tool.id, ... }`). No data-layer change needed — the flag is already on the DTO.

5. **Hide-empty toggle**:
   - New query param `hideEmpty=1` (default unset = false). Same shape as the existing enum params in `usePanoramaState.ts`.
   - Filter applied in `app/pages/mcp/panorama.vue` inside `filteredGroups.filterTools` — drop tools whose `mcps.every((m) => m.isPlaceholder)` when `hideEmpty.value` is true. The existing `mcps.length > 0` drop at the end of that function handles cascade removal of empty PDTs and empty groups automatically.
   - UI control sits in `SectionHeader.vue` between the status-chip row and the view-mode toggle. A small labeled chip with an `EyeOff` icon (lucide-vue-next) — active-state styling matches the StatusChip "active" look so it reads as a filter. Label text via new i18n key.

6. **i18n key**: `mcpPanorama.filter.hideEmpty` — "Hide empty" / "隐藏空工具".

## Changes

### `app/composables/usePanoramaState.ts`
- Add `hideEmpty = computed<boolean>(() => route.query.hideEmpty === "1")`.
- Add `setHideEmpty(value: boolean)` that calls `push({ hideEmpty: value ? "1" : null })`.
- Export both from the composable.

### `app/pages/mcp/panorama.vue`
- Destructure `hideEmpty, setHideEmpty` from `usePanoramaState()`.
- Extend `filterTools` inside `filteredGroups`:
  ```ts
  .filter((tool) => !hideEmpty.value || tool.mcps.some((m) => !m.isPlaceholder))
  ```
  Place after the existing `inDrill` filter, before the per-MCP status filter map.
- Pass `hideEmpty.value` to `SectionHeader` and bind `@update:hide-empty="setHideEmpty"`.

### `app/components/mcp-landscape/SectionHeader.vue`
- Add `hideEmpty: boolean` prop and `update:hideEmpty: [boolean]` emit.
- Import `EyeOff` from `lucide-vue-next`.
- New toggle button placed in the controls row just before the view-mode pill group (`ml-auto` stays on the view-mode group; this new button sits to its left). Active-state styling: `bg-(--color-card) text-(--color-ink) shadow-…` like the active view-mode button. Inactive: muted. Label: `t("mcpPanorama.filter.hideEmpty")`. Aria-pressed mirrors the bool.

### `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue`
- Sector tool-row pill block (currently `w-[16px] h-[6px] rounded-full` …):
  - Wrap the `<button>` in `v-if="!mcp.isPlaceholder"` (skip placeholders entirely).
  - Replace size + color classes with `w-[28px] h-[14px] rounded-full inline-flex items-center justify-center text-[9px] font-mono font-medium`.
  - Background + text via per-status pair: `bg-(--color-status-released-bg) text-(--color-status-released)` / `bg-(--color-status-dev-bg) text-(--color-status-dev)`. Add a tiny helper `pillBgTextClass(status)` next to `dotClass`.
  - Inner text: `MCP` for released, `Dev` for dev.
  - Drop the `mcp.isPlaceholder && 'opacity-40'` class (no longer needed since placeholders aren't rendered).
- PDT tool-row pill block: same treatment with `w-[24px] h-[12px]` and `text-[8px]`. Adjust `gap-[2px]` between pills to `gap-1` for better breathing room at the new size.
- The existing `dotClass` helper stays (currently unused after this change inside OverviewView, but the helper itself can remain — or remove if no other call sites). Quick grep confirms it's only used in this one file's pill rendering, so it can be deleted along with the old class strings.

### `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json`
- Add `mcpPanorama.filter.hideEmpty`: `"Hide empty"` / `"隐藏空工具"`.

## Verification

1. `bun run lint` — clean.
2. `bun run typecheck` — exit 0.
3. `bun run test` — 256/256.
4. **Manual browser check (user-side)** at `bun run dev`:
   - `/en/mcp/panorama` (default) — pills now show "MCP" / "Dev" text; tools that had only a placeholder show their name with no pill beside it.
   - Click a "MCP" pill → existing detail panel opens, still navigates to the marketplace link.
   - Click the new "Hide empty" toggle → tool rows for no-MCP tools disappear; URL gains `?hideEmpty=1`. Click again → they return; URL drops the param.
   - `/zh/mcp/panorama` — confirm "隐藏空工具" label.
   - `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` modes — confirm those still render their existing pill chips unchanged (the changes here are scoped to OverviewView only; the regular `McpTile.vue` is untouched).

## Critical files

- `app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue` (pill rewrite + placeholder skip)
- `app/components/mcp-landscape/SectionHeader.vue` (new toggle button, prop, emit)
- `app/composables/usePanoramaState.ts` (`hideEmpty` query param + setter)
- `app/pages/mcp/panorama.vue` (filterTools extension; pass through to header)
- `i18n/locales/en.json` and `i18n/locales/zh.json` (1 new key)
- Reference only (no edits): `app/components/mcp-landscape/McpTile.vue` (pill styling reference), `server/utils/queries/mcp-landscape.ts` (`placeholderMcp` synthesis confirms the data shape we're filtering on), `app/assets/css/tailwind.css` (status color tokens).

## Commit & branch flow

- Stay on `feat/panorama-overview-mode`. One commit covering both visual + filter changes since they're tightly coupled (the toggle becomes much more useful once placeholders aren't visible by default).
- Conventional commit: `feat(mcp-panorama): labeled Overview pills + hide-empty toggle`. No `Co-Authored-By:` trailer.
- Do not push automatically — wait for explicit "push it" from the user.

file.content:
<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  groupDisplayTitle,
  pdtDisplayTitle,
  toolDisplayName,
  type DomainGroup,
  type Group,
  type Layer,
  type McpDto,
  type ToolDto,
} from "~~/shared/mcp-panorama"

const props = defineProps<{
  layer: Layer
  groups: Group[]
  activeMcpId: number | null
}>()
const emit = defineEmits<{
  pick: [{ tool: ToolDto; mcp: McpDto }]
  drill: [string]
}>()

const { locale, t } = useI18n()

type BucketKey = "rnd" | "product" | "other"

// Fixed top-to-bottom order on the public layer.
const BUCKET_ORDER: BucketKey[] = ["rnd", "product", "other"]

// Maps the 5 known public-layer domain keys to their super-bucket.
// Unknown keys fall through to "other" so a new domain added to the seed
// shows up rather than silently disappearing.
const BUCKET_FOR: Record<string, BucketKey> = {
  "ai-rd": "rnd",
  "prod-sw-eng": "product",
  "hw-eng": "other",
  "prod-digi": "other",
  "rnd-facilities": "other",
  "prod-config": "other",
}

interface OverviewBucket {
  key: BucketKey
  title: string
  items: DomainGroup[]
}

// Partition the public-layer domain groups into the three buckets, in fixed
// order. Empty buckets are dropped so a status filter that wipes out an
// entire bucket doesn't leave an orphan section header.
const buckets = computed<OverviewBucket[]>(() => {
  if (props.layer !== "public") return []
  const groupsByBucket: Record<BucketKey, DomainGroup[]> = {
    rnd: [],
    product: [],
    other: [],
  }
  for (const g of props.groups) {
    if (g.kind !== "domain") continue
    const bucket = BUCKET_FOR[g.key] ?? "other"
    groupsByBucket[bucket].push(g)
  }
  return BUCKET_ORDER.filter((k) => groupsByBucket[k].length > 0).map((k) => ({
    key: k,
    title: t(`mcpPanorama.bucket.${k}`),
    items: groupsByBucket[k],
  }))
})

function dotClass(status: McpDto["status"]): string {
  switch (status) {
    case "released":
      return "bg-(--color-status-released)"
    case "dev":
      return "bg-(--color-status-dev)"
    default:
      return "bg-(--color-status-none)"
  }
}

input.command:
git add app/components/mcp-landscape/OverviewView.vue app/components/mcp-landscape/SectionHeader.vue app/composables/usePanoramaState.ts app/pages/mcp/panorama.vue i18n/locales/en.json i18n/locales/zh.json && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(mcp-panorama): labeled Overview pills + hide-empty toggle

Two coordinated changes to the Overview view:

- Each per-MCP pill now carries a short label inside ("MCP" for
  released, "Dev" for in-development) using the same bg-tint / saturated
  foreground pair McpTile uses. Pills bumped to 28x14 (sector) and
  24x12 (PDT) so the mono text reads at 9px / 8px. Placeholder MCPs
  (synthesized server-side for tools with no real MCPs) no longer
  render at all — the tool name shows up bare instead of paired with a
  dimmed dot, which makes the coverage gap obvious without adding
  visual noise.

- A new "Hide empty" toggle in SectionHeader (EyeOff icon, placed left
  of the view-mode pill group) drops every tool row whose only MCP is
  the placeholder. Default off; state is persisted in the URL as
  `?hideEmpty=1` alongside the other panorama query params. The filter
  hooks into the existing `filterTools` in panorama.vue, so cascade
  removal of newly-empty PDTs and groups works for free.

i18n: new mcpPanorama.filter.hideEmpty key in en + zh.
EOF
)" && git log --oneline -2

content.text:
Committed `7eff1c4` on `feat/panorama-overview-mode`. Local branch is 1 commit ahead of `origin`.

## What changed

6 files / +76 / −37:

- **`OverviewView.vue`** — `dotClass` swapped for `pillClass` + `pillLabel` helpers. Both pill blocks (sector `w-[28px] h-[14px]` / PDT `w-[24px] h-[12px]`) now render as labeled chips with text "MCP" / "Dev" inside (mono, 9px / 8px). Placeholder MCPs gated by `v-if="!mcp.isPlaceholder"` — they don't render at all instead of being shown dimmed. Hover scale reduced to `1.15` since the larger pills don't need the big enlargement. Active-state ring kept, dropped the `scale-[1.4]` boost since the pill itself is now legible.
- **`usePanoramaState.ts`** — new `hideEmpty` computed (`route.query.hideEmpty === "1"`) + `setHideEmpty(value)` setter. Persisted via the same `push()` pattern as the other params.
- **`SectionHeader.vue`** — new `hideEmpty: boolean` prop + `update:hideEmpty: [boolean]` emit. EyeOff icon + label button placed to the left of the view-mode pill group, active-state styling matches the active view-mode button.
- **`panorama.vue`** — destructures `hideEmpty` + `setHideEmpty` from the composable, adds the filter line inside `filterTools` (after `inDrill`, before the status filter), passes both into `SectionHeader`. Cascade removal of empty PDTs/groups falls out for free from the existing `tool.mcps.length > 0` drop at the end of `filterTools`.
- **`i18n/locales/{en,zh}.json`** — added `mcpPanorama.filter.hideEmpty` ("Hide empty" / "隐藏空工具").

## Verification

- `bun run lint` clean
- `bun run typecheck` exit 0
- `bun run test` 256/256

## For browser check

- `/en/mcp/panorama` — pills now show "MCP" / "Dev"; no-MCP tools have a bare name with no pill beside it
- Click the "Hide empty" toggle — those bare-name tool rows disappear; URL gains `?hideEmpty=1`
- `/zh/mcp/panorama` — confirm "隐藏空工具" label
- `?view=panorama` and `?view=list` — confirm those modes still render unchanged

When you're ready, say "push it" and I'll push commit `7eff1c4` so PR #39 picks it up.
